attractions.name: Alameda Park
attractions.description: Alameda Park is one of Santa Barbara's oldest and most iconic parks, spanning two city blocks in the downtown area. It is renowned for its diverse collection of rare trees and the massive 8,000-square-foot Kids' World wooden playground. The park serves as a central gathering place for major community events, including the Summer Solstice and Earth Day festivals
attractions.address: 1400 Santa Barbara St, Santa Barbara, CA 93101
attractions.hours: Monday: Sunrise - 10:00 PM, Tuesday: Sunrise - 10:00 PM, Wednesday: Sunrise - 10:00 PM, Thursday: Sunrise - 10:00 PM, Friday: Sunrise - 10:00 PM, Saturday: Sunrise - 10:00 PM, Sunday: Sunrise - 10:00 PM
highlights: Kids' World Playground: A massive 8,000-square-foot wooden play structure with a castle, slides, and swings
Rare Tree Collection: Home to a variety of unique and rare trees from around the world, featured in a self-guided tree tour
Historic Bandstand: A wooden gazebo originally built in 1888 for public entertainment and concerts
Festival Hub: The primary site for large-scale city celebrations such as the Summer Solstice Parade festival and Earth Day
Picnic Areas: Multiple reservable sites including the Castle, Whale, and Harriet Miller picnic areas
attractions.tips: Parents should supervise children closely at Kids' World as the large structure has many hidden areas. Be mindful of street cleaning signs (often on Tuesdays) to avoid parking tickets. The park is very shady and ideal for hot days, though some areas may have a transient population
attractions.nearby: Alice Keck Park Memorial Gardens (directly across the street), Santa Barbara County Courthouse, Santa Barbara Public Library, State Street shopping and dining

attractions.name: Alice Keck Park Memorial Garden
attractions.description: Often called the "crown jewel" of Santa Barbara's city parks, Alice Keck Park Memorial Garden is a 4.6-acre botanical oasis occupying an entire downtown block. It features a diverse collection of 75 tree and plant species, a picturesque koi and turtle pond, and winding walking paths. The garden also includes a unique sensory garden designed for visually and physically impaired visitors, offering audio tours and Braille signs
attractions.address: 1500 Santa Barbara St, Santa Barbara, CA 93101
attractions.hours: Sunrise to 10:00 pm daily
highlights: Koi and turtle pond with water lilies and a wooden gazebo
Sensory garden with audio posts and Braille signs for impaired visitors
Botanical collection featuring 75 different tree and plant species
Low-water use demonstration garden
Wisteria arbor area popular for wedding ceremonies
Butterfly garden with native plantings
attractions.tips: Street parking is available in the surrounding neighborhood, though be mindful of 90-minute time limits on nearby blocks. It is a peaceful spot perfect for reading or a quiet picnic, but visitors should bring their own food as options are limited on-site. Dogs are welcome but must be kept on a leash at all times
attractions.nearby: Alameda Park, Kids World (playground), Santa Barbara County Courthouse, Unitarian Society of Santa Barbara, Old Mission Santa Barbara

attractions.name: Barbareño
attractions.description: Barbareño is a refined yet casual restaurant in downtown Santa Barbara that celebrates the unique flavors and traditions of California's Central Coast. The menu features creative interpretations of local specialties, such as the Santa Maria BBQ and the 'Eggamuffin,' using high-quality ingredients from regional purveyors. Guests can enjoy their meals in a modern dining room or on a cozy, heated outdoor patio, complemented by a selection of premier California wines and craft beers
attractions.address: 205 W. Canon Perdido St., Santa Barbara, CA 93101
attractions.hours: Sunday - Saturday: 5:00 PM - 9:00 PM (some sources indicate Fri/Sat until 9:30 PM)
highlights: Specializes in 'Central Coast Cuisine' inspired by local history and traditions
Famous for the 'Eggamuffin,' a gourmet twist on the iconic breakfast sandwich
Features a dedicated 'Santa Maria BBQ' platter, honoring the region's wood-grilled tradition
Recognized by the MICHELIN Guide for its bold, flavorful dishes and whimsical attention to detail
Offers an extensive list of local California wines and craft beers
Heated outdoor patio dining available for a comfortable year-round experience
attractions.tips: Reservations are highly recommended as the restaurant is popular and fills up quickly. For a unique dessert experience, try the 'Baked California,' a local favorite. Street parking is available nearby but can be busy during peak hours
attractions.nearby: El Presidio de Santa Barbara Historic State Park, Santa Barbara County Courthouse, Santa Barbara Public Market, Susan Quinlan Doll & Teddy Bear Museum & Library

attractions.name: Bibi Ji
attractions.description: Bibi Ji offers a modern and approachable twist on traditional Indian cuisine, highlighting local produce and seafood in a vibrant, eclectic atmosphere. The restaurant features a critically-acclaimed menu paired with an extensive list of low-intervention, sustainable, and organically farmed natural wines
attractions.address: 1213 State Street, Suite B, Santa Barbara, CA 93101
attractions.hours: Monday: 5:00 PM – 10:00 PM; Tuesday: Closed; Wednesday: Closed; Thursday: 5:00 PM – 10:00 PM; Friday: 5:00 PM – 10:00 PM; Saturday: 5:00 PM – 10:00 PM; Sunday: 5:00 PM – 10:00 PM
attractions.admission: Varies (Small plates and entrees typically range from $31-$50; special Thali Night on Mondays is $39)

Extensive natural wine list curated by James Beard award-winning sommelier Rajat Parr
Signature dishes like the Uni Biryani (sea urchin biryani) and Crispy Cauliflower
Vibrant, modern dining room with a "pastel-pink" aesthetic and energetic atmosphere
Weekly specials including Thali Night (Mondays) and Locals Night
Chef's Counter tasting experiences and private dining options
attractions.tips: Reservations are highly recommended, especially for weekends; the atmosphere is energetic and can be quite loud during peak hours. For the best experience, try the Uni Biryani if it is available, as its availability depends on local catches
attractions.nearby: Santa Barbara Museum of Art, Santa Barbara County Courthouse, Granada Theatre, Arlington Theatre, Alice Keck Park Memorial Gardens

attractions.name: Bouchon Santa Barbara
attractions.description: Bouchon is an upscale dining destination specializing in "Wine Country Cuisine" that bridges French culinary techniques with the fresh, seasonal bounty of the Central Coast. The restaurant offers a warm, inviting ambience with a menu that changes frequently to highlight ingredients from the Santa Barbara Channel and local farms. It is widely recognized for its exceptional wine list and romantic atmosphere, making it a staple of the Santa Barbara fine dining scene
attractions.address: 9 West Victoria St, Santa Barbara, CA 93101
attractions.hours: Sunday-Thursday: 5:00 PM - 9:00 PM (Last seating 8:30 PM); Friday-Saturday: 5:00 PM - 10:00 PM
highlights: Exquisite California-French "Wine Country Cuisine" using locally sourced ingredients
Award-winning local wine list with a "Wine Spectator Award of Excellence"
Unique 'Market Tour & Gourmet Dinner' experiences on Tuesdays and Saturdays
Elegant private dining options including the "Cork Room" and terrace buyouts
Consistently rated as one of the most romantic restaurants in America
AAA Four Diamond rating for over a decade
attractions.tips: Reservations are highly recommended, especially for weekend dining and the popular Market Tours. For parking, use City Lot #5 located directly behind the restaurant (entry from Chapala Street), which offers the first 75 minutes free
attractions.nearby: Santa Barbara Public Market, The Granada Theatre, Santa Barbara County Courthouse, State Street Shopping District, Santa Barbara Art Works

attractions.name: Carr Vineyards & Winery
attractions.description: Established in 1999, Carr Vineyards & Winery is a full-production winery and tasting room located in a historic 1940s Quonset Hut in downtown Santa Barbara. The winery specializes in ultra-premium, limited-production wines sourced exclusively from Santa Barbara County vineyards, including Pinot Noir, Syrah, and Cabernet Franc. Visitors can enjoy the unique ambiance of a working winery while sipping on handcrafted wines, with regular art shows and live music enhancing the industrial-chic atmosphere
attractions.address: 414 N Salsipuedes St, Santa Barbara, CA 93103-3127
attractions.hours: Sunday: 12:00 PM – 7:00 PM, Monday: 12:00 PM – 7:00 PM, Tuesday: 12:00 PM – 7:00 PM, Wednesday: 12:00 PM – 7:00 PM, Thursday: 12:00 PM – 8:00 PM, Friday: 12:00 PM – 9:00 PM, Saturday: 12:00 PM – 8:00 PM
attractions.admission: $25 for Standard Tasting (5 pour flight), $35 for Specialty Tasting (5 pour flight); $5 tap wine on Thirsty Thursdays from 5 PM to close. Flight is free with purchase of 3 bottles
highlights: Located in a historic 1940s Quonset Hut with an industrial ambiance
Full-production winery where guests can see the winemaking process
Specializes in limited-production wines like Pinot Noir, Syrah, and Cabernet Franc
Dog-friendly outdoor patio with a fire pit
Regular live music performances and monthly art shows
Thirsty Thursdays featuring $5 glasses of wine on tap
attractions.tips: Walk-ins are welcome, but reservations are recommended for parties of 6 or more. The venue becomes 21+ only after 5:00 PM daily. Street parking is typically available right out front, making it an easier alternative to the busier Funk Zone
attractions.nearby: Cutler's Artisan Spirits, Jaffurs Wine Cellars, The Funk Zone (approx. 0.6 mi), Santa Barbara County Courthouse (approx. 0.8 mi), Metro 4 Theatres

attractions.name: Casa de la Guerra
attractions.description: Built between 1818 and 1828 for José de la Guerra, the fifth comandante of the Presidio, this adobe residence served as the social, political, and cultural center of Santa Barbara during the Mexican period. Today, it is a museum and historic landmark managed by the Santa Barbara Trust for Historic Preservation, featuring furnished period rooms and rotating exhibits on California’s Hispanic heritage
attractions.address: 15 East De la Guerra Street, Santa Barbara, CA 93101
attractions.hours: Thursday: 12:00 PM – 4:00 PM, Friday: 12:00 PM – 4:00 PM, Saturday: 12:00 PM – 4:00 PM, Sunday: 12:00 PM – 4:00 PM, Monday – Wednesday: Closed
attractions.admission: $5 for adults, $4 for seniors (62+), Free for SBTHP members, SNAP/EBT and CalFresh cardholders, and children 16 and under
highlights: Authentic 19th-century adobe architecture and a peaceful central courtyard
Furnished period rooms including a sala (living room), office, bedroom, and store
Rotating historical exhibits such as "Santa Barbara 1925 – 2025: A Portrait in Maps"
The on-site museum gift shop featuring local historical items
Designation as a City Landmark, California Landmark, and National Register of Historic Places site
attractions.tips: Tickets purchased at Casa de la Guerra also include admission to El Presidio de Santa Bárbara State Historic Park. Nearby public parking is available at 115 E. Canon Perdido Street via the PayByPhone app
attractions.nearby: De La Guerra Plaza, El Presidio de Santa Bárbara State Historic Park, Santa Barbara Historical Museum, Museum of Contemporary Art Santa Barbara, State Street shopping district

attractions.name: Corazon Cocina
attractions.description: Corazon Cocina offers a vibrant culinary journey through the flavors of Mexico, specializing in unique and creative interpretations of street food style tacos and ceviche. Chef Ramon Velazquez uses locally sourced, seasonal ingredients and traditional culinary techniques to serve "food from the heart" in a hip, energetic atmosphere. Located inside the Santa Barbara Public Market, it has become a staple of the local dining scene and is recognized with a Michelin Bib Gourmand award
attractions.address: 38 W Victoria St #122, Santa Barbara, CA 93101
attractions.hours: Monday: 11:00 AM - 9:00 PM; Tuesday: 11:00 AM - 9:00 PM; Wednesday: 11:00 AM - 9:00 PM; Thursday: 11:00 AM - 9:00 PM; Friday: 11:00 AM - 9:00 PM; Saturday: 11:00 AM - 9:00 PM; Sunday: 11:00 AM - 9:00 PM
highlights: Michelin Bib Gourmand recipient for exceptional cuisine at moderate prices
Inventive regional Mexican street food including tacos, quesadillas, and award-winning ceviche
House-made blue corn tortillas and fresh salsas prepared daily
Located within the bustling Santa Barbara Public Market with a fun, social atmosphere
Chef Ramon Velazquez’s cooking has been featured on the Cooking Channel’s “Taco Trip with Chef Sanchez”
attractions.tips: Visit during off-peak hours to avoid long lines, especially during the busy lunch rush. Don't miss the Tuesday Special for discounted Al Pastor tacos, and always say "yes" to the trio of complimentary salsas served with your order
attractions.nearby: Santa Barbara Public Market, The Arlington Theatre, Santa Barbara County Courthouse, Santa Barbara Museum of Art, State Street

attractions.name: Dune Coffee Roasters
attractions.description: Dune Coffee Roasters is a local favorite specialty coffee shop and roastery that emphasizes community, quality, and accessibility. Founded in 2009, they source and roast their own beans in Santa Barbara to provide a unique and approachable coffee experience
attractions.hours: Monday: 7:00 AM - 4:00 PM, Tuesday: 7:00 AM - 4:00 PM, Wednesday: 7:00 AM - 4:00 PM, Thursday: 7:00 AM - 4:00 PM, Friday: 7:00 AM - 4:00 PM, Saturday: 7:00 AM - 4:00 PM, Sunday: 7:00 AM - 4:00 PM

Flagship location on the historic corner of State St and Figueroa
Participates in the 'Choose to Reuse' program with cup discounts
Expertly crafted espresso drinks and seasonal offerings
Airy, modern atmosphere perfect for a break while shopping on State Street
attractions.tips: Bring your own reusable cup to receive a discount on your drink through their sustainability initiatives. The State Street location offers great outdoor seating for people-watching in the heart of downtown
attractions.nearby: Santa Barbara Museum of Art, The Historic La Arcada Courtyard, Bella Rosa Galleries, Satellite Wine Bar & Cafe

attractions.name: El Presidio de Santa Bárbara State Historic Park
attractions.description: El Presidio de Santa Bárbara State Historic Park is a 5.5-acre site that preserves the last Spanish fortress, or presidio, founded in Alta California in 1782. The park features restored and reconstructed adobe buildings, including the chapel and commandant's quarters, offering a glimpse into Santa Barbara's early military and cultural history
attractions.address: 123 E Canon Perdido St, Santa Barbara, CA 93101
attractions.hours: Monday: 10:30 a.m. – 4:30 p.m., Tuesday: 10:30 a.m. – 4:30 p.m., Wednesday: 10:30 a.m. – 4:30 p.m., Thursday: 10:30 a.m. – 4:30 p.m., Friday: 10:30 a.m. – 4:30 p.m., Saturday: 10:30 a.m. – 4:30 p.m., Sunday: 10:30 a.m. – 4:30 p.m
attractions.admission: $5 for adults, $4 for seniors (62+), Free for SBTHP members, SNAP/EBT and CalFresh cards, and children 16 & under. Includes admission to Casa de la Guerra
highlights: Reconstructed Presidio Chapel and Comandancia
Original 1782 adobe buildings: El Cuartel and the Cañedo Adobe
Whittaker Orchard and Heritage Gardens
Exhibits on the Japanese American community and early Spanish life
Jimmy’s Oriental Gardens historic site
Guided tours available on weekends
attractions.tips: Begin your visit at the Visitor Center in the Cañedo Adobe for an orientation and a tour booklet. Your admission ticket also provides entry to the nearby Casa de la Guerra museum. Consider joining the public guided tour at 2 p.m. on Saturdays and Sundays for a more in-depth experience
attractions.nearby: Casa de la Guerra, Lobero Theatre, Santa Barbara County Courthouse, El Paseo, Downtown Post Office

The venue offers a laid-back atmosphere with both indoor and outdoor patio seating, often hosting live music and trivia nights. The restaurant offers a stylish yet relaxed atmosphere for everything from "Bubbles & Brunch" to elegant dinners and daily happy hours

attractions.description: Located in the historic El Paseo in Santa Barbara’s Presidio Neighborhood, this award-winning tasting room offers a warm, elegant setting featuring estate-grown Bordeaux varietals. Guests can enjoy tastings on sun-soaked patios in the historic El Paseo courtyard or inside by a cozy fireplace. The winery is family-owned and known for its focus on Cabernet Sauvignon and Sauvignon Blanc from the Happy Canyon AVA
attractions.hours: Monday: 12:00 PM - 5:00 PM, Tuesday: 12:00 PM - 5:00 PM, Wednesday: 12:00 PM - 5:00 PM, Thursday: 12:00 PM - 5:00 PM, Friday: 12:00 PM - 5:00 PM, Saturday: 12:00 PM - 5:00 PM, Sunday: 12:00 PM - 5:00 PM (Last daily seating at 5:00 PM)

Sustainable farming practices and reclaimed wood interior from the Columbia River

attractions.description: Handlebar Coffee Roasters is an artisanal coffee shop and micro-roastery founded by former professional cyclists. It offers fresh-roasted beans, specialty espresso drinks, and local pastries in a bike-themed atmosphere. The shop is a popular community hub known for its high-quality, sustainable coffee and welcoming environment
attractions.address: 128 E Canon Perdido St, Santa Barbara, CA 93101

Signature espresso drinks including the seasonal Gibraltar blend

attractions.nearby: El Presidio de Santa Barbara State Historic Park, Santa Barbara Historical Museum, Lobero Theatre

attractions.name: Inspiration Point Trail
attractions.description: Inspiration Point is one of Santa Barbara's most popular hiking destinations, offering a 3.4-mile round-trip trek to a summit 1,800 feet above the coastline. The trail provides breathtaking 180-degree panoramic views of the city, the Pacific Ocean, and the Channel Islands. It is considered moderately challenging with a steady climb through oak woodlands and chaparral
attractions.address: 1498 Tunnel Rd (Main Access) or 1150 San Roque Rd
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours (Daylight hiking recommended)
highlights: Panoramic 180-degree views of Santa Barbara and the Channel Islands
Two access points: Tunnel Road (3.5 miles RT) or San Roque Road
Dog-friendly and mountain bike-friendly trail conditions
Connectivity to other popular spots like Seven Falls and Mission Canyon Trail
Scenic passage through oak-filled canyons and rugged sandstone formations
attractions.tips: Arrive early, especially on weekends, as parking at the Tunnel Road trailhead is extremely limited and heavily ticketed. The trail is largely exposed, so bring plenty of water, sunscreen, and wear sturdy hiking boots for the steep and rocky sections
attractions.nearby: Seven Falls, Santa Barbara Botanic Garden, Old Mission Santa Barbara, Rattlesnake Canyon Park

attractions.name: Jesusita Trail
attractions.description: The Jesusita Trail is a scenic 7-mile out-and-back path that winds through the lush riparian habitats of San Roque Canyon and the chaparral-covered foothills of Santa Barbara (). It follows a gurgling creek shaded by oak and sycamore trees before ascending to panoramic vistas at Inspiration Point (). The trail is a popular choice for hikers, trail runners, and mountain bikers seeking sweeping views of the city and Channel Islands ()
attractions.address: San Roque Rd (just past the Cater Water Treatment Plant), Santa Barbara, CA 93105
attractions.hours: Monday: Open 24 Hours, Tuesday: Open 24 Hours, Wednesday: Open 24 Hours, Thursday: Open 24 Hours, Friday: Open 24 Hours, Saturday: Open 24 Hours, Sunday: Open 24 Hours ()
highlights: Panoramic views of Santa Barbara, Lauro Canyon Reservoir, and the Channel Islands at Inspiration Point ()
Shaded oak woodlands and lush riparian habitat along Jesusita Creek ()
Multiple creek crossings that provide wading spots for dogs ()
Connections to Stevens Park and the historic Tunnel Trail ()
Seasonal displays of wildflowers and sightings of local wildlife like lizards ()
Challenging switchbacks leading to high-elevation vistas ()
attractions.tips: Wear sturdy hiking shoes with good traction as the trail is rocky and can be slippery near the creek (). Bring plenty of water and sun protection, especially for the exposed upper sections of the climb (). Be cautious of poison oak, which is common along the shaded portions of the trail (). Arrive early to secure limited parking near the Cater Water Treatment plant (). Mountain bikers should use bells to alert hikers and other trail users ()
attractions.nearby: Stevens Park, Inspiration Point, Seven Falls, Santa Barbara Botanic Garden, Tunnel Trail, Lauro Canyon Reservoir (,)

attractions.name: Julienne
attractions.description: Julienne was a premier farm-to-table and nose-to-tail bistro in downtown Santa Barbara, celebrated for its sophisticated California-French cuisine. The restaurant emphasized meticulously sourced local ingredients and house-made specialties, including an extensive charcuterie program. During its eight-year run, it earned significant acclaim from critics and food enthusiasts alike before closing in 2016
attractions.address: 138 E Canon Perdido St, Santa Barbara, CA 93101
attractions.hours: Wednesday – Saturday: 5:00 PM – 10:00 PM; Sunday: 5:00 PM – 9:00 PM
attractions.admission: Varies; tasting menus typically ranged from $45 to $85 per person, with a la carte options also available
highlights: Farm-to-table dining with ingredients hand-selected from local Santa Barbara Farmers Markets
Renowned house-made charcuterie program featuring items like saucisson sec and finocchiona
Weekly and sometimes nightly changing seasonal menus
Nose-to-tail culinary philosophy incorporating unique cuts such as bone marrow and sweetbreads
Acclaimed multi-course tasting menus (3, 5, or 7 courses) with expert wine pairings
Award-winning establishment, including winning a national James Beard Foundation contest
attractions.tips: The charcuterie plate is widely considered a mandatory order for first-time visitors. The atmosphere is casual and unpretentious, so formal attire is not required despite the high-end food. For the best experience, opt for the five-course tasting menu with wine pairings
attractions.nearby: El Presidio de Santa Bárbara State Historic Park, Lobero Theatre, Santa Barbara Historical Museum

attractions.name: La Arcada Plaza
attractions.description: La Arcada Plaza is a historic European-style courtyard in the heart of Santa Barbara's Arts District, featuring tile-lined walkways, fountains, and lush greenery. Designed in 1926 by architect Myron Hunt, this charming destination offers a curated collection of locally owned boutiques, galleries, and restaurants. It is renowned for its interactive public art, including lifelike bronze sculptures and a popular turtle fountain
attractions.hours: Open daily; individual shop, gallery, and restaurant hours vary
highlights: Historic Spanish Colonial Revival architecture designed by Myron Hunt in 1926
Interactive public art collection including lifelike bronze sculptures by J. Seward Johnson and George Lundeen
Iconic Turtle Fountain featuring real red-eared slider turtles
State Street Clock Tower inspired by a 1880 Tiffany's design
Quaint European-style courtyard with seven restaurants and independent boutiques
Historic 1741 Spanish Galleon cannon and 1761 original Santa Barbara Mission bell
attractions.tips: Parking is convenient at nearby city lots, which often offer the first 75 minutes free. Families should visit the main fountain to see the turtles basking in the sun. Check the official website for a "Treasure Hunt" guide to find all 22 hidden art pieces in the courtyard
attractions.nearby: Santa Barbara County Courthouse, Santa Barbara Museum of Art, Arlington Theatre, State Street Promenade, Granada Theater

attractions.name: Lobero Theatre
attractions.description: The Lobero Theatre is a historic 604-seat performing arts venue in downtown Santa Barbara, recognized as California's oldest continuously operating theater (). Originally built in 1873 and rebuilt in 1924, it hosts a wide variety of community and professional productions, including music, dance, and theater (). It is celebrated for its intimate atmosphere and exceptional acoustics, making it a favorite for both local artists and international stars ()
attractions.address: 33 E. Canon Perdido St., Santa Barbara, CA 93101
attractions.hours: Box Office: Monday–Friday, 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM; Performance Days: Box Office opens 2 hours prior to curtain; Bar services open 1 hour prior to curtain ()
attractions.days_open: Box Office: Monday–Friday; Theater: Open on performance days (including weekends) ()
attractions.admission: Ticket prices vary by performance; most tickets include a $7.00 facility fee. Tickets can be purchased at the box office, by phone, or online (web and phone orders incur a $3.00 fee per ticket) ()
highlights: California’s oldest continuously operating theater and a registered California Historic Landmark ()
Intimate 604-seat venue with celebrated acoustics and a Spanish Colonial Revival design ()

Rich history of hosting legendary performers from jazz icons to Hollywood stars ()
Certified "Green Theater" committed to sustainability and reducing waste ()
attractions.tips: Purchase tickets directly through the official website to ensure validity (). Parking is conveniently located in City Lot #9 off Anacapa Street, though street parking is also available nearby (). The theater encourages visitors to bring their own reusable water bottles or pint cups as part of their green initiative ()
attractions.nearby: El Presidio de Santa Barbara State Historic Park, Santa Barbara County Courthouse, State Street shopping and dining, Santa Barbara Historical Museum ()

The venue features a spacious interior, a front patio for people-watching, and a back patio with its own bar for special events. Guests can enjoy a wide variety of award-winning ales and lagers alongside burgers and Mexican street food from the on-site "Downtown Eats" kitchen

attractions.name: MOVE Santa Barbara County
attractions.description: MOVE Santa Barbara County (formerly the Santa Barbara Bicycle Coalition) is a countywide advocacy organization that promotes safe bicycling and walking through education and infrastructure. They maintain a comprehensive network of local trails and routes, ranging from the iconic beachfront Cabrillo Bike Path to challenging mountain climbs like Gibraltar Road. The organization also operates Bici Centro, a community DIY bike repair shop that empowers residents with maintenance skills and affordable refurbished bicycles. (,)
attractions.hours: Organization/Shop: Tue-Fri 11:30 AM - 5:30 PM, Sat 12:00 PM - 4:00 PM (Trails: Sunrise to Sunset) ()
attractions.admission: Free to use trails; DIY bike shop services typically cost around $5/hour for tools and assistance. (,)
highlights: Cabrillo Bike Path: An iconic 4.5-mile scenic coastal route connecting Leadbetter Beach to Butterfly Beach. ()
Obern Trail: A 10.5-mile paved path providing a car-free connection between Goleta Beach and the Santa Barbara waterfront. ()
Bici Centro: A community DIY bicycle repair shop offering tools, parts, and expert guidance for affordable maintenance. ()
Gibraltar Road Climb: Known as the 'Alp d'Huez of Santa Barbara,' offering a world-class challenge for serious road cyclists. ()
State Street Promenade: A car-free downtown stretch perfect for social rides, shopping, and dining. ()
Hope Ranch Loop: A local favorite featuring quiet, tree-shaded roads and stunning residential architecture. ()
attractions.tips: For the best experience on the Cabrillo Bike Path, ride during weekday mornings to avoid weekend crowds. If you're tackling the mountain climbs like Gibraltar, ensure you bring plenty of water and check your brakes for the steep descent. Use the interactive bike network map on the organization's website to plan custom routes that utilize dedicated bike lanes and paths. (,)
attractions.nearby: Stearns Wharf, Santa Barbara Zoo, East Beach, Chase Palm Park, State Street Promenade ()

attractions.name: McConnell's Fine Ice Creams
attractions.description: McConnell's Fine Ice Creams is a historic Santa Barbara institution founded in 1949 that specializes in made-from-scratch ice cream using local, sustainable, and organic ingredients. The company maintains a 70-year legacy of producing dense, pure-flavored treats without preservatives, stabilizers, or additives of any kind
attractions.hours: Sun-Thurs: 11:00 am - 10:00 pm; Fri-Sat: 11:00 am - 11:00 pm
highlights: Made-from-scratch ice cream using local, grass-grazed milk
Inventive regional flavors like Eureka Lemon & Marionberries and Sea Salt Cream & Cookies
House-baked cookies and custom ice cream sandwiches
Five-scoop ice cream flights for sampling multiple flavors
Historic legacy as a third-generation, family-run business
attractions.tips: The lines can be long during peak times, but they move quickly and the wait is worth it; consider a flight to try multiple flavors at once. Seating is limited inside the State Street shop, but there is plenty of outdoor seating available in the nearby plaza
attractions.nearby: The Salt Cave, Casa de la Guerra, and various shops and restaurants along the State Street Promenade

attractions.name: Milk & Honey Tapas
attractions.description: Established in 2007, Milk & Honey is a premier craft cocktail and tapas bar located in the heart of downtown Santa Barbara. The establishment offers a modern take on traditional Spanish-inspired small plates, featuring a diverse menu of creative dishes like PB&J sliders and bacon-wrapped dates. Known for its intimate, dimly lit atmosphere and award-winning cocktails, it serves as a popular destination for both early happy hours and late-night dining
attractions.address: 30 W Anapamu St, Santa Barbara, CA 93101
attractions.hours: Monday: 5:00 PM - 10:00 PM, Tuesday: 5:00 PM - 10:00 PM, Wednesday: 5:00 PM - 10:00 PM, Thursday: 5:00 PM - 11:00 PM, Friday: 5:00 PM - 12:00 AM, Saturday: 5:00 PM - 12:00 AM, Sunday: 5:00 PM - 9:00 PM
attractions.days_open: Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday
attractions.admission: Varies (Tapas prices range approximately from $9 to $28 per dish, with larger entrees like Paella at $50)
highlights: Extensive menu of creative Spanish-inspired tapas such as 'Date With A Pig' and 'PB&J Sliders'
Award-winning craft cocktail program featuring unique drinks like the Violet Margarita
Cozy and intimate atmosphere with low lighting and a 'loungy' vibe
Daily Happy Hour from 5:00 PM to 6:30 PM and Late Night Happy Hour on Thursday through Saturday
Outdoor seating available with a gas-lit fireplace and heaters for cooler evenings
Late-night dining options with the kitchen open until 12:00 AM on Fridays and Saturdays
attractions.tips: Reservations are highly recommended due to the restaurant's popularity and intimate size, though walk-ins are also welcome. For the best value, visit during one of their two happy hour windows, especially the late-night one for a hip evening vibe. Parking is conveniently available right behind the restaurant, with the first 75 minutes free in the city lot
attractions.nearby: Karpeles Manuscript Library, Don Q Family Billiard Center, Kimpton Canary Hotel, Bouchon Santa Barbara, Arigato Sushi

attractions.name: Municipal Winemakers Santa Barbara
attractions.description: Municipal Winemakers is a modern, unpretentious winery focused on producing vibrant and expressive wines from Santa Barbara County. Founded by Dave Potter, the winery features multiple labels including the approachable Muni line and single-vineyard Potek bottlings. After 15 years in the Funk Zone, the Santa Barbara tasting room has relocated to a pop-up space at its sister winery, Potek, within The Mill complex
attractions.address: 406 E. Haley Street, Santa Barbara, CA 93101
attractions.hours: Sun-Wed: 12:00 PM – 8:00 PM; Thu-Sat: 12:00 PM – 9:00 PM
highlights: Features three distinct labels: Muni (everyday), Potek (single-vineyard), and Nowadays
Located in "The Mill," a refurbished historic feed mill industrial complex
Shared outdoor courtyard with Third Window Brewery and Kitchen
Offers in-depth, seated single-vineyard tastings in the lounge
Pet-friendly and kid-friendly atmosphere with a relaxed, hip vibe
attractions.tips: Reservations are highly recommended for seated tastings in the lounge on weekends, while walk-ins are typically welcome for casual glasses on the patio
attractions.nearby: Third Window Brewing, Potek Winery, Third Window Kitchen, Santa Barbara Surf Museum

attractions.name: Old Mission Santa Barbara ()
attractions.description: Known as the 'Queen of the Missions,' Old Mission Santa Barbara is a living national historical landmark and active Franciscan friary founded in 1786. The site features a historic church, a museum of colonial-era artifacts, a mausoleum, and 15 acres of lush gardens overlooking the Pacific Ocean. (,)
attractions.hours: Monday - Saturday: 9:30 AM - 5:00 PM; Sunday: 12:30 PM - 5:00 PM (Last tour ticket sold at 4:00 PM) ()
attractions.days_open: Daily (Closed Easter Sunday, Thanksgiving Day, and Christmas Day) ()
attractions.admission: Adults (18-64): $17.00, Seniors (65+): $15.00, Active Military (w/ID): $15.00, Youth (5-17): $12.00, Children (4 & Under): Free, Museums for All (SNAP EBT): $3.00 ()
highlights: Historic Mission Church with iconic twin bell towers ()
Nine-room museum showcasing colonial-era artwork and artifacts ()
Historic Cemetery and Mausoleum where notable figures are interred ()
15 acres of beautiful grounds and gardens with ocean views ()
A.C. Postel Memorial Rose Garden located directly in front of the Mission ()
Home to an active community of Franciscan Friars since its founding ()
attractions.tips: Self-guided tours include an audio guide available in multiple languages; allocate 1 to 2 hours for a full visit. It is an active church, so some areas like the sanctuary or cemetery may be closed for masses, weddings, or funerals. Free parking is available in the front parking lot off Los Olivos Street. (,)
attractions.nearby: A.C. Postel Memorial Rose Garden, Santa Barbara Museum of Natural History, Rocky Nook Park, Alice Keck Park Memorial Gardens, Santa Barbara Botanic Garden (,)

attractions.name: Olio e Limone Ristorante
attractions.description: Olio e Limone Ristorante is a family-owned establishment in downtown Santa Barbara's Arts District specializing in artisanal Italian cuisine. The name means "oil and lemon," reflecting a philosophy of relying on ingredient integrity and quality preparation for a "simply sophisticated" dining experience. The restaurant features an inviting atmosphere with European hospitality and an extensive, award-winning wine cellar
attractions.address: 11 West Victoria Street, Suite 17, Santa Barbara, CA 93101
attractions.hours: Lunch: Monday–Saturday, 11:30 AM – 2:00 PM; Dinner: Nightly from 5:00 PM
attractions.days_open: Open daily for dinner; Monday through Saturday for lunch
highlights: Artisanal Italian cuisine featuring house-made pastas like duck ravioli and Sicilian spaghetti with bottarga
Extensive wine list with over 230 selections, recipient of the Wine Spectator Award of Excellence since 2002
Private dining in the Cucina Room, which can accommodate up to 40 guests for special events
Located in a historic building within the downtown Santa Barbara Arts District
Offers unique organic extra-virgin olive oil made from Sicilian varieties for purchase
attractions.tips: Reservations are highly recommended, particularly for dinner or before local theater performances. Visitors can also explore the sister establishments next door, including Olio Pizzeria and Olio Bottega for casual dining and Italian retail items
attractions.nearby: Granada Theatre, Santa Barbara Center for the Performing Arts, State Street Promenade

attractions.name: Paseo Nuevo Shops & Restaurants
attractions.description: Paseo Nuevo is a premier outdoor shopping destination and the heart of downtown Santa Barbara, offering over 50 national retailers, specialty shops, and restaurants. The center features beautiful Spanish-style architecture with courtyards, blooming gardens, and romantic pathways dazzled with Moroccan lights after sunset. It serves as a vibrant community hub blending shopping, dining, and lifestyle experiences like local theater and contemporary art
attractions.hours: Monday: 11am - 7pm, Tuesday: 11am - 7pm, Wednesday: 11am - 7pm, Thursday: 11am - 7pm, Friday: 11am - 7pm, Saturday: 11am - 7pm, Sunday: 11am - 7pm
highlights: Over 50 national retailers, specialty shops, and local boutiques
Outdoor Spanish-style architecture with lush courtyards and tiled steps
Center Stage Theater and Museum of Contemporary Art Santa Barbara
Variety of dining options including California Pizza Kitchen and Eureka!
Year-round community events, live entertainment, and seasonal celebrations
Convenient downtown parking with 75 minutes of free parking
attractions.tips: Enjoy the first 75 minutes of parking for free in the Paseo Nuevo parking structure accessed via Chapala Street. Visit the colorful Spanish-tiled steps for a popular photo opportunity. Use the public restrooms located on Level 2, and check the event calendar for 'Sounds on State' live music. Wear comfortable walking shoes as the mall is an open-air pedestrian-friendly environment
attractions.nearby: State Street Promenade, Casa de la Guerra, De La Guerra Plaza, Lobero Theatre, Santa Barbara Historical Museum, Stearns Wharf

attractions.name: Rattlesnake Canyon Trail
attractions.description: Rattlesnake Canyon Trail is a popular 4.5-mile out-and-back trail in the Santa Barbara foothills, following a year-round creek through shaded oak groves and chaparral. The trail offers moderate elevation gain, picturesque water features, and expansive views of the Pacific Ocean and Santa Ynez Mountains. Despite its name, the trail is known for its winding, serpentine path rather than an abundance of snakes
attractions.address: Las Canoas Road, Santa Barbara, CA 93105
attractions.hours: Open daily, year-round; Day use only
highlights: Year-round creek with cascading waterfalls and deep pools
Remnants of a historic stone dam built in 1808
Panoramic ocean and mountain views from Tin Can Meadow and Gibraltar Road
Shaded trail sections featuring oak trees, sycamores, and a small pine grove
Connects to Tunnel Trail and Gibraltar Road for longer hiking loops
attractions.tips: Wear sturdy hiking shoes for creek crossings and rocky sections. Keep a vigilant eye for poison oak along the trail edges. Dogs are allowed but must be kept on a leash. Arrive early on weekends as parking along Las Canoas Road is limited and strictly enforced
attractions.nearby: Skofield Park, Santa Barbara Botanic Garden, Old Mission Santa Barbara, Tunnel Trail, Gibraltar Road

attractions.name: Rocky Nook Park
attractions.description: Rocky Nook Park is a serene 19-acre sylvan oasis nestled among towering oak and sycamore trees overlooking Mission Creek. This historic park is celebrated for its massive sandstone boulders, which provide a natural playground for climbing, and its tranquil, shaded atmosphere. It offers a peaceful retreat for picnicking and light hiking just minutes from the city's historic core
attractions.address: 610 Mission Canyon Rd, Santa Barbara, CA 93105
attractions.hours: 8:00 AM - Sunset daily
highlights: Massive sandstone boulders perfect for climbing
Shaded groves of ancient oak and sycamore trees
Short nature trails and scenic walking paths
Children's playground and horseshoe pits
Reservable picnic areas with BBQ grills
Seasonal access to Mission Creek
attractions.tips: The park is an ideal spot for families, as the large boulders offer excellent natural climbing opportunities for children. While general admission is free, picnic areas are popular and can be reserved for a fee. Parking is available on-site but may fill up during weekends and holidays
attractions.nearby: Old Mission Santa Barbara, Santa Barbara Museum of Natural History, Santa Barbara Botanic Garden, Mission Rose Garden

attractions.name: Sama Sama Kitchen
attractions.description: Sama Sama Kitchen is a Michelin Bib Gourmand restaurant in downtown Santa Barbara that specializes in Indonesian-inspired Southeast Asian cuisine. Co-owned by chefs Ryan Simorangkir and Tyler Peek, the eatery combines traditional street food flavors with fresh, locally sourced ingredients from Santa Barbara farms. The restaurant is known for its vibrant atmosphere, creative cocktails, and family-style sharing plates
attractions.hours: Monday: Closed; Tuesday: 12:00 PM - 3:00 PM, 5:00 PM - 8:30 PM; Wednesday: 12:00 PM - 3:00 PM, 5:00 PM - 8:30 PM; Thursday: 12:00 PM - 3:00 PM, 5:00 PM - 8:30 PM; Friday: 12:00 PM - 3:00 PM, 5:00 PM - 9:30 PM; Saturday: 12:00 PM - 3:00 PM, 5:00 PM - 9:30 PM; Sunday: 12:00 PM - 3:00 PM, 5:00 PM - 8:30 PM
highlights: Michelin Bib Gourmand award recipient for high-quality, good-value cooking
Famous signature wings available in sweet or spicy Vietnamese styles
Charming hidden back patio featuring string lights and communal seating
Innovative craft cocktail program highlighting Southeast Asian ingredients like calamansi and yuzu
Authentic Indonesian staples such as Mie Goreng, Nasi Goreng, and Gado Gado
Farm-to-table philosophy using seasonal produce from local farmers' markets
attractions.tips: Reservations are highly recommended, especially for the popular outdoor patio. Don't miss the Crispy Rice Salad and Signature Wings, which are among the most highly-rated dishes. The restaurant is dog-friendly on the patio, making it a great spot for pet owners
attractions.nearby: The Granada Theatre, Sullivan Goss An American Gallery, The Good Lion, Santa Barbara Art District

attractions.name: Sandbar Cocina y Tequila
attractions.description: Sandbar Cocina y Tequila is a vibrant restaurant and bar located in the heart of Santa Barbara's historic State Street. The venue blends classic Spanish architecture with the breezy elegance of a Mexican coastal escape, featuring a sun-soaked outdoor patio and contemporary hacienda setting. Guests can enjoy a menu of elevated street tacos, fresh seafood, and an extensive program of premium tequilas and mezcals
attractions.hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 AM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 AM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 AM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 AM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 AM, Saturday: 9:00 AM – 2:00 AM, Sunday: 9:00 AM – 2:00 AM
attractions.days_open: Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday
highlights: Coastal-inspired Mexican cuisine including birria tacos and fresh ceviche
Extensive tequila and mezcal bar with handcrafted specialty cocktails
Large sun-soaked outdoor patio on the State Street promenade
Vibrant late-night destination with DJs and dancing seven nights a week
Contemporary hacienda interior with custom coastal cabana design
Weekly Mexican-inspired brunch and daily happy hour specials
attractions.tips: Visit during Happy Hour (daily 3pm-7pm) for "buy one cocktail, get the second for a quarter" specials. Arrive on Saturdays and Sundays between 9am and 1pm to catch their specialized Mexican brunch menu. The venue transforms into a high-energy dance spot late at night, so it is recommended to arrive early for a more relaxed dining experience
attractions.nearby: Stearns Wharf, The Red Piano, Hotel Santa Barbara, Institution Ale Co., 3D Studio Gallery

attractions.name: Santa Barbara Botanic Garden
attractions.description: The Santa Barbara Botanic Garden is a 78-acre "living museum" dedicated exclusively to the conservation and display of California's diverse native flora. Founded in 1926, the garden features over 1,000 species of rare and indigenous plants set against the dramatic backdrop of the Santa Ynez Mountains. It serves as a world-renowned center for plant science, education, and sustainable horticulture
attractions.address: 1212 Mission Canyon Rd, Santa Barbara, CA 93105
attractions.hours: General Hours: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM (Daily); Members' Hour: 9:00 AM – 10:00 AM (Daily); Nursery & Shop: 9:30 AM – 4:30 PM (Daily)
attractions.admission: Peak Season (Mar 1 – May 31): Adults $25, Seniors (60+) & Military $21, Students $21, Youth (3-12) $17; Regular Season: Adults $23, Seniors & Military $18, Students $18, Youth $14; Children under 3: Free; Museums for All: $3. Dogs are free
highlights: 11 distinct sections representing California ecosystems
Historic Mission Dam and Aqueduct built in 1806
Over five miles of walking trails with views of the Channel Islands
Shin Ka An Japanese Teahouse and Garden
Pritzlaff Conservation Center for scientific research
Public Native Plant Nursery
attractions.tips: Advanced reservations are required for all non-member visitors. Wear comfortable walking shoes for the five miles of trails, and bring water for the more primitive areas. Leashed dogs are welcome and admitted for free. Check the official website for Red Flag Warning closures during critical fire weather
attractions.nearby: Old Mission Santa Barbara, Santa Barbara Museum of Natural History, Rattlesnake Canyon Trail, Rocky Nook County Park, State Street / Downtown Santa Barbara

attractions.name: Santa Barbara County Courthouse
attractions.description: The Santa Barbara County Courthouse is an iconic Spanish Colonial Revival landmark completed in 1929 following a devastating earthquake. It serves as a working courthouse while functioning as a premier tourist attraction known for its white stucco walls, red tile roofs, and lush sunken gardens. The building is celebrated as one of the most beautiful government structures in the United States and is a National Historic Landmark. (,)
attractions.hours: Monday – Friday: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM; Saturday – Sunday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM (Visitors not admitted after 4:30 PM) ()
highlights: Mural Room: Features 4,200 square feet of hand-painted murals depicting California history by Dan Sayer Groesbeck ()
El Mirador Clock Tower: An 85-foot observation deck offering 360-degree views of the city, mountains, and ocean ()
Sunken Gardens: A beautifully landscaped 1.5-acre outdoor space used for community events and weddings ()
Bisno Schall Clock Gallery: Displays the rare, fully operational 1929 Seth Thomas clock mechanism ()
Spirit of the Ocean Fountain: A famous sandstone sculpture at the main entrance ()
Ornate Tile Work: Hand-glazed Tunisian tiles and intricate decorative elements throughout the interior ()
attractions.tips: Allow extra time for security screening, which is similar to airport security but generally does not require shoe removal. Visit at 10:30 AM or 2:00 PM for a free docent-led tour to learn the building's deep history. Tripods and selfie sticks are prohibited inside the building. (,)
attractions.nearby: Santa Barbara Museum of Art, Santa Barbara Public Library, Lobero Theatre, El Presidio de Santa Barbara State Historic Park ()

attractions.name: Santa Barbara Historical Museum
attractions.description: Founded in 1932, the Santa Barbara Historical Museum is located in the heart of the city's historic district and is dedicated to preserving the rich heritage of the region. The museum's extensive collections represent over 500 years of history, including artifacts from the Chumash, Spanish, Mexican, and American periods. Visitors can explore the signature 'Story of Santa Barbara' exhibition and historic adobes on the museum's campus
attractions.address: 136 East De la Guerra Street, Santa Barbara, CA 93101
attractions.hours: Monday: Closed; Tuesday: Closed; Wednesday: 12:00 PM - 5:00 PM; Thursday: 12:00 PM - 7:00 PM; Friday: 12:00 PM - 5:00 PM; Saturday: 12:00 PM - 5:00 PM; Sunday: 12:00 PM - 5:00 PM
highlights: 'The Story of Santa Barbara' signature exhibition
Historic Covarrubias and Historic Adobes buildings located onsite
Gledhill Research Library featuring rare maps, photographs, and manuscripts
Extensive collection of Western saddles and equestrian artifacts
Period costumes and decorative arts representing 500 years of local history
Recognized as a premier cultural landmark and popular wedding venue
attractions.tips: Admission is free, though donations are encouraged to support preservation efforts. A free parking lot is available for visitors adjacent to the museum entrance on De la Guerra Street. Consider visiting on the first Thursday of each month for '1st Thursday' after-hours events featuring music and wine from 5:00 PM to 8:00 PM
attractions.nearby: El Presidio de Santa Barbara State Historic Park, Casa de la Guerra, State Street Shopping & Dining, McConnell's Fine Ice Creams

attractions.name: Santa Barbara Museum of Art
attractions.description: The Santa Barbara Museum of Art (SBMA) is a leading art museum located in downtown Santa Barbara, California. Founded in 1941, it houses a permanent collection of over 27,000 works spanning 5,000 years of human history, including significant holdings in Asian, American, and European art
attractions.hours: Tuesday – Sunday: 11 am – 5 pm; 1st Thursdays: 11 am – 8 pm; Closed Monday
attractions.days_open: Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday
attractions.admission: General Admission: Adult $15, Senior (65+) $10, Youth (6-17) $6, Child (under 6) Free, Members Free. Featured Exhibitions: Adult $30, Senior (65+) $25, Youth (6-17) $21. Free Admission: 1st Thursday (5 – 8 pm)
highlights: 19th-century French art including works by Monet and Renoir
Extensive Asian art collection featuring Buddhist art and contemporary photography
Significant 19th and 20th-century American paintings and works on paper
Newly renovated galleries with interactive Family Resource Center programming
Masterpieces by artists such as Picasso, Matisse, and Van Gogh
attractions.tips: Advance ticket reservations are highly recommended. Take advantage of free admission on the first Thursday of every month from 5 pm to 8 pm. Use the provided QR codes in galleries for in-depth information on portraits
attractions.nearby: Old Mission Santa Barbara, Santa Barbara County Courthouse, State Street shopping district, Paseo Nuevo, and the Granada Theatre

attractions.name: Santa Barbara Museum of Natural History
attractions.description: Founded in 1916, this natural history museum features ten indoor exhibit halls focusing on regional biodiversity, including astronomy, birds, mammals, and marine life. The 17-acre campus is nestled in oak woodlands along Mission Creek and is famous for its iconic 72-foot blue whale skeleton and the Central Coast's only full-dome planetarium
attractions.address: 2559 Puesta del Sol, Santa Barbara, CA 93105-2936
attractions.hours: Monday–Sunday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM. (Closed Thanksgiving, Christmas Day, and New Year's Day; closes early at 3:00 PM on Christmas Eve)
attractions.days_open: Open daily (Monday–Sunday), excluding major holidays and special event closures
attractions.admission: Members: Free; Adult (18–64): $20; Senior (65+): $17; Teen (13–17): $17; Child (2–12): $15; Child under 2: Free; SNAP/Cal Fresh (EBT): $5 per person (up to 4 people). Note: Some special exhibits like Butterflies Alive! may require an additional $3 ticket
highlights: Iconic 72-foot articulated Blue Whale skeleton at the entrance
Central Coast's only full-dome planetarium featuring live shows
Prehistoric Forest with life-size, animatronic dinosaurs
Extensive collection of Chumash Indian artifacts and dioramas
Butterflies Alive! seasonal walk-through pavilion
17 acres of nature trails and oak woodlands along Mission Creek
attractions.tips: Parking is free in the spacious lot. Pack a lunch to enjoy at one of the many outdoor seating areas by the creek. While advanced booking is not required, reservations are recommended for popular exhibits like Butterflies Alive!
attractions.nearby: Old Mission Santa Barbara, Santa Barbara Botanic Garden, Rocky Nook Park, and the Sea Center (sister site on Stearns Wharf)

attractions.name: Santa Barbara Public Market
attractions.description: The Santa Barbara Public Market is a contemporary urban food hall located in the heart of downtown Santa Barbara's performing and cultural arts district. It features a diverse collection of merchants offering locally and regionally sourced food, wine, and beer in a bright, LEED-certified space. The market serves as a premiere dining destination where visitors can experience the bounty of California's Central Coast. (,)
attractions.address: 38 West Victoria Street, Santa Barbara, CA 93101
attractions.hours: Monday - Wednesday: 11:00 AM - 9:00 PM; Thursday - Saturday: 11:00 AM - 10:00 PM; Sunday: 11:00 AM - 9:00 PM ()
highlights: Diverse collective of local and regional food merchants ()
LEED-certified contemporary urban design ()
Home to popular vendors like Corazon Cocina and Empty Bowl Gourmet Noodle Bar ()
Full bar service available at Cooney's Bar ()
Private event space available at 28Vic ()
Indoor and outdoor dining options ()
attractions.tips: Individual merchant hours may vary from the general market schedule. Convenient public parking is available in nearby city lots where the first 75 minutes are free. Visitors can take advantage of free public Wi-Fi (SBPM Public WiFi) and the market is an ideal choice for groups with diverse culinary preferences. (,)
attractions.nearby: Santa Barbara County Courthouse, State Street Promenade, Arlington Theatre, Granada Theatre, SOhO Restaurant and Music Club (,)

attractions.name: Santa Barbara Wine Therapy
attractions.description: Santa Barbara Wine Therapy is a laid-back, dog-friendly wine bar located in the heart of historic downtown Santa Barbara. It offers a relaxed atmosphere where patrons can enjoy local wines, craft beers, and signature house specialties like Frosé and Sangria. The venue features a spacious outdoor patio, a variety of board games, and even a special menu for canine companions
attractions.address: 12 W Haley St, Santa Barbara, CA 93101
attractions.hours: Monday: Closed; Tuesday: Closed; Wednesday: 5:00 PM – 11:00 PM; Thursday: 5:00 PM – 11:00 PM; Friday: 5:00 PM – 12:00 AM; Saturday: 1:00 PM – 12:00 AM; Sunday: 1:00 PM – 11:00 PM
highlights: Exceptionally dog-friendly environment with a dedicated menu and treats for pets
Extensive selection of local Santa Barbara wines and locally brewed craft beers
House specialty drinks including freshly made Frosé and Sangria
Large, inviting outdoor patio space perfect for social gatherings
Fun atmosphere featuring a wide range of board and card games like Jenga and Uno
Social hub located in beautiful downtown Santa Barbara
attractions.tips: Dogs are not just welcome but celebrated here; be sure to ask for the special dog menu and treats. The bar allows you to bring your own food or order delivery from nearby restaurants to enjoy on the patio. The Frosé (frozen rosé) is highly recommended, especially on warm afternoons. It's a great spot for groups to hang out and play the various board games provided
attractions.nearby: Night Lizard Brewing Company, Santa Barbara Certified Farmers Market, The Lark Santa Barbara, McConnell's Fine Ice Creams, and the historic State Street shopping district

attractions.name: Santa Barbara Winery
attractions.description: Founded in 1962 by Pierre Lafond, Santa Barbara Winery is the oldest post-prohibition commercial winery in the county. Located in the heart of the vibrant Funk Zone, it offers a wide range of award-winning wines produced using sustainable practices. The winery is a pioneer of the region's wine industry and remains a cornerstone of the downtown tasting scene
attractions.hours: Monday: 12:00 PM – 6:00 PM; Tuesday: 12:00 PM – 6:00 PM; Wednesday: 12:00 PM – 6:00 PM; Thursday: 12:00 PM – 6:00 PM; Friday: 12:00 PM – 6:00 PM; Saturday: 12:00 PM – 6:00 PM; Sunday: 12:00 PM – 6:00 PM
attractions.admission: Tasting fees start at $15; other experiences may vary
highlights: Oldest winery in Santa Barbara County, established in 1962
Located in the heart of the eclectic Funk Zone neighborhood
Pioneered the planting of Pinot Noir and Syrah in the region
Certified sustainable winegrowing practices
Walk-in tastings welcome; no reservations required
Production and storage facility located on-site in downtown
attractions.tips: No reservations are required for visiting and tasting, making it perfect for spontaneous visits. The winery offers items suitable for an impromptu beach picnic, and it is located just two blocks from the beach. Sharing tastings is a great way to sample more varieties responsibly
attractions.nearby: The Lark, Loquita, Stearns Wharf, MOXI Science Museum, Kunin Wines, Municipal Winemakers

attractions.name: Seven Falls Trail
attractions.description: Seven Falls is a cherished natural landmark in the Santa Ynez Mountains, featuring a series of seven cascading waterfalls sculpted from sandstone. The trail winds through Mission Canyon, following the west fork of Mission Creek and offering natural swimming holes and lush greenery. It is a popular front-country hike that involves rock hopping and light scrambling to reach the upper falls. (,)
attractions.hours: Open daily 24 hours (Year-round) ()
highlights: Series of seven cascading waterfalls and sandstone pools ()
Natural swimming holes and "potholes" carved into the rock ()
Panoramic views of the Santa Ynez Mountains and coastline ()
Adventure-filled route with rock scrambling and boulder hopping ()
Lush canyon environment with ferns and moss-covered rock faces ()
Connection to the popular Inspiration Point viewpoint ()
attractions.tips: Arrive early to secure extremely limited parking on Tunnel Road and stay behind white lines to avoid tickets or towing. Use a GPS track as the trail is unmarked and includes steep, slippery sections with ropes for assistance. For the best waterfall flow and swimming conditions, visit in the spring or shortly after a significant rainfall. (,)
attractions.nearby: Inspiration Point, Santa Barbara Botanic Garden, Old Mission Santa Barbara, Jesusita Trail, Tunnel Trail (,)

attractions.name: State Street Promenade
attractions.description: State Street is the historic social and cultural heart of Santa Barbara, stretching from the foothills to the Pacific Ocean. Its central 10-block section has been transformed into a vibrant pedestrian-only promenade filled with outdoor dining, boutiques, and historic architecture. The street is renowned for its Mediterranean and Spanish Colonial Revival style, lush landscaping, and lively atmosphere that seamlessly connects downtown to the waterfront. (,)
attractions.hours: Street access: Open 24 hours daily. Typical business hours: Monday – Sunday, 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM (individual shop and restaurant hours vary). (,)
highlights: State Street Promenade: A 10-block car-free pedestrian zone for walking and biking
Spanish Colonial Revival Architecture: Iconic red-tiled roofs and white stucco buildings
Santa Barbara Museum of Art: A premier cultural institution featuring classical and contemporary works

attractions.name: The Arlington Theatre
attractions.description: The Arlington Theatre is Santa Barbara's largest movie theater and principal performing arts venue, boasting a seating capacity of over 2,000 guests. Built in 1931 on the site of the former Arlington Hotel, the historic landmark features a magnificent Spanish Colonial and Mission Revival design. Its interior is famous for its atmospheric 'Spanish Village' setting, complete with a star-filled 'sky' and intricate architectural details

attractions.days_open: Daily, with events and movie screenings occurring throughout the week

Atmospheric interior designed as an Andalusian village with twinkling stars on the ceiling

Inspired by the coastal landscapes of France and Italy, the area features winding streets, historic stone terracing, and a sunny climate that often sits above the city's marine layer. It serves as a quintessential example of the "American Riviera" lifestyle, blending historical charm with luxury residential living

It serves as a vital habitat for over 200 species of resident and migratory birds and offers passive recreation like birdwatching and hiking. The refuge is a key part of the East Cabrillo Boulevard Parkway Historic District, providing scenic views near the waterfront

attractions.name: Arroyo Burro Beach County Park ()
attractions.description: Arroyo Burro Beach, known locally as Hendry’s Beach, is a popular destination featuring a long stretch of sandy coastline flanked by picturesque coastal bluffs. It is renowned for its dog-friendly atmosphere, offering both on-leash and off-leash areas, as well as opportunities for swimming, surfing, and bird watching. The park also serves as the terminus for Arroyo Burro Creek and is home to an ecological education center (,)
attractions.hours: Monday - Sunday: 8:00 AM to Sunset ()
highlights: Off-leash dog area located east of the creek ()
Boathouse at Hendry's Beach restaurant with ocean views ()
Self-service dog wash station ($10 fee) ()
Watershed Resource Center for ecological education ()
Free-of-charge beach wheelchairs available seasonally ()
Access to the adjacent Douglas Family Preserve trails ()
attractions.tips: Arrive early, especially on weekends, as the parking lot fills up quickly. Visit during low tide to enjoy a long walk along the bluffs toward Goleta or the harbor. If you bring a dog, remember that the off-leash area is to the left (east) of the creek mouth, while the right side requires leashes (,)

The park also includes a grassy picnic area and is home to a well-known oceanfront restaurant

It houses an impressive collection of over 40 restored horse-drawn vehicles and more than 50 historic saddles, many of which are still used in local parades like the Old Spanish Days Fiesta

Historic Los Olivos stagecoach and other rare Western artifacts including tack and memorabilia

Free parking is available in the lot adjacent to the museum and the nearby baseball fields. For a more in-depth experience, docent-led tours can be booked in advance by appointment

Each trip is guided by expert naturalists from the Channel Islands Naturalist Corps, providing educational insights into the Santa Barbara Channel's diverse ecosystem. The vessel features a luxury teak-paneled cabin, a full galley with food and beverages, and multiple viewing decks

attractions.description: The Douglas Family Preserve is a 70-acre public park featuring undeveloped oceanfront land and the largest coastal open space within the city limits of Santa Barbara (). Donated to the city in 1997 with a significant contribution from actor Michael Douglas, the preserve offers scenic trails through diverse ecosystems including oak woodlands and coastal bluffs (). It is a beloved destination for passive recreation, offering panoramic views of the Pacific Ocean and the Channel Islands ()

Monday: Sunrise to sunset
Tuesday: Sunrise to sunset
Wednesday: Sunrise to sunset
Thursday: Sunrise to sunset
Friday: Sunrise to sunset
Saturday: Sunrise to sunset
Sunday: Sunrise to sunset ()
