Parking can be difficult in the Funk Zone, so consider using public lots like City Lot 13 near the Amtrak station. The bakery is counter-service; place your order first and then find a seat at the communal tables or on the patio. (,)
attractions.nearby: Santa Barbara Wine Collective, MOXI (The Wolf Museum of Exploration + Innovation), Stearns Wharf, The Lark, Lucky Penny, Figueroa Mountain Brewing Co. (,)

attractions.name: Kayak Santa Barbara ()
attractions.description: Kayak Santa Barbara offers premier guided tours and rentals with over 35 years of expertise in the Santa Barbara Harbor. Visitors can enjoy an immersive journey along the scenic coastline, featuring breathtaking views of the city and the majestic Santa Ynez Mountain range. The tours provide opportunities to encounter diverse marine life, including sea lions, seals, and various coastal birds ()
attractions.address: 301 W Cabrillo Blvd, Santa Barbara, CA 93101 ()
attractions.hours: Daily: 10:00 AM – 2 hours before sunset ()
attractions.admission: Guided Tours: From $79 per adult, $59 per child. Rentals: ~ $22.77 for singles (2 hrs), ~ $41.40 for tandems (2 hrs) (,)
highlights: Expert-led Sunset Kayak Tours ()
Close-up wildlife viewing of Sea Lions and Harbor Seals ()
Panoramic views of the Santa Ynez Mountains and Santa Barbara skyline ()
Wide selection of single and tandem kayak equipment ()
Conveniently located at the Santa Barbara Landing near public boat ramps ()
Over three decades of local kayaking and water safety experience ()
attractions.tips: Arrive at least 15 minutes before your scheduled tour or rental time. Bring a swimsuit and towel as you are likely to get wet during the activity. Ensure all participants are able to swim, as it is a requirement for water tours. Booking in advance online is recommended, particularly for popular sunset tours (,)
attractions.nearby: Stearns Wharf, Santa Barbara Harbor, Leadbetter Beach, Santa Barbara Maritime Museum (,)

attractions.name: Leadbetter Beach Park
attractions.description: Leadbetter Beach is a scenic south-facing city beach and park located between the Santa Barbara Harbor and Shoreline Park. It is a popular destination for beginning surfers, windsurfers, and catamaran sailors, as well as joggers and sunbathers. The 17.92-acre site features a large grassy lawn, reservable picnic areas, and on-site dining at the Shoreline Beach Cafe
attractions.hours:
Monday: Sunrise to 1/2 hour after sunset
Tuesday: Sunrise to 1/2 hour after sunset
Wednesday: Sunrise to 1/2 hour after sunset
Thursday: Sunrise to 1/2 hour after sunset
Friday: Sunrise to 1/2 hour after sunset
Saturday: Sunrise to 1/2 hour after sunset
Sunday: Sunrise to 1/2 hour after sunset
attractions.admission: Free. Parking: $3.50/hour (max $20/day) at the Leadbetter lot
highlights: Popular spot for beginning surfers and windsurfers
Large grassy park with reservable picnic and BBQ sites
Direct access to the Shoreline Beach Cafe for beachfront dining
Proximity to the Santa Barbara Harbor and Santa Barbara City College
Outdoor showers and public restrooms available
Scenic views of the coastline and Santa Ynez Mountains
attractions.tips: Arrive early on weekends as the parking lot and beach can get very crowded. If the main lot is full, additional parking and a scenic overlook are available on the roadway uphill from the beach. Note that dogs are not permitted on the sand at Leadbetter Beach
attractions.nearby: Santa Barbara Harbor, Shoreline Park, Santa Barbara City College, Stearns Wharf, Santa Barbara Maritime Museum

attractions.name: Lucky Penny
attractions.description: Lucky Penny is a stand-alone restaurant in the heart of Santa Barbara’s Funk Zone featuring innovative artisanal pizzas from a wood-burning oven. The eatery is famously known for its exterior tiled with more than 150,000 real copper pennies that shimmer in the sunshine, offering a casual and artistic dining experience with locally sourced salads and sweet treats
attractions.hours: Sunday - Thursday: 11:30 AM - 8:00 PM; Friday - Saturday: 11:30 AM - 9:00 PM
highlights: Exterior tiled with 150,000+ real copper pennies
Artisanal pizzas crafted in a wood-burning oven
Lively outdoor picnic patio seating
Located in the heart of the artsy Funk Zone district
Signature "Frosé," local craft beer, and wine selection
Seasonal "Market Pizza" and locally sourced garden-fresh salads
attractions.tips: Order at the walk-up window; seating is first-come, first-served on the outdoor patio. The restaurant is kid and pet-friendly, making it a great casual spot for families
attractions.nearby: The Lark, MOXI Museum, Stearns Wharf, Santa Barbara Wine Collective, Urban Wine Trail

attractions.name: Moreton Bay Fig Tree
attractions.description: The Moreton Bay Fig Tree is a massive historical landmark believed to be the largest of its species in the United States, with a canopy spread of over 170 feet. Planted in 1877 from an Australian seedling, it has become a community fixture that provides enough shade to cover approximately 16,000 people. It was officially designated as a tree of notable historic interest in 1970 and remains a popular site for photography and nature enthusiasts
attractions.hours: Sunrise to 1/2 hour after Sunset
highlights: Believed to be the largest Ficus macrophylla (Moreton Bay Fig) in the United States
Massive branch spread of over 175 feet and a height of approximately 80 feet
Planted on July 4, 1876, by a local girl and transplanted to its current location in 1877)
Provides more than 21,000 square feet of shade
Designated as an official historic landmark and a "tree of notable historic interest"
attractions.tips: Visit during daylight hours for the best views and safety, as the area is not well-lit at night. Use the adjacent Amtrak station parking lot, which offers free parking for up to 90 minutes. Be aware that the tree's root system is currently protected by a chain-link fence, which may slightly impact photography from certain angles
attractions.nearby: Santa Barbara Amtrak Station, The Funk Zone, MOXI (The Wolf Museum of Exploration + Innovation), Stearns Wharf, Reagan Ranch Center

attractions.name: Santa Barbara Harbor
attractions.description: Santa Barbara Harbor is a historic working harbor and recreational hub located along the city's scenic waterfront. It serves as a premier launch pad for water-based adventures, including kayaking, sailing, and whale watching. The area also features a variety of local restaurants, shops, and the Santa Barbara Maritime Museum
attractions.hours: Harbor area: Open 24 hours daily; Waterfront Parking: 8:00 a.m. – 10:00 p.m. daily (,)
highlights: Home to the Santa Barbara Maritime Museum and Outdoors Santa Barbara Visitor Center
Launch point for boat charters, whale watching, and sunset cruises
Fresh seafood dining at local favorites like Brophy Bros. and On The Alley
Weekly Saturday Fishermen's Market with fresh local catch
Scenic strolls along the breakwater wall for views of the mountains and ocean
Recreational rentals for kayaking, paddleboarding, and jet skiing
attractions.tips: The first 90 minutes of parking are free in many harbor spots, but for high-demand events, it is best to arrive early. Don't miss the 360-degree views from the 4th floor of the Maritime Museum or a ride on the 'Lil Toot' water taxi to Stearns Wharf
attractions.nearby: Stearns Wharf, Leadbetter Beach, West Beach, Shoreline Park, Santa Barbara Maritime Museum

attractions.name: Santa Barbara Maritime Museum ()
attractions.description: The Santa Barbara Maritime Museum serves as a hub for the region's coastal heritage, highlighting 13,000 years of maritime history from Chumash culture to modern surfing movements (). It is an interactive facility that provides hands-on learning opportunities and engaging exhibitions for all ages within the Santa Barbara Harbor ()
attractions.address: 113 Harbor Way, Suite 190, Santa Barbara, CA 93109 ()
attractions.hours: Daily: 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM. Closed on Thanksgiving, Christmas, New Year's Day, and the first Friday in August ()
attractions.admission: Adult (18-64): $12, Senior (65+): $8, Student w/ ID: $6, Youth (6-17): $6, Child (5 and under): Free. Discounted admission of $2 for EBT cardholders via Museums for All ()
highlights: Point Conception Lighthouse First Order Fresnel Lens ()
Munger Theater showing daily high-definition films ()
Interactive Sport Fishing exhibit and Jack Tarr boating game ()
Outdoors Santa Barbara Visitor Center on the 4th floor with panoramic views ()
Exhibits on Chumash culture, shipwrecks, and commercial diving ()
attractions.tips: Visit the 4th-floor visitor center for the best views of the harbor and coastline (). Check for Groupon deals to save on admission costs (). Free 90-minute parking is available in the nearby harbor lot ()
attractions.nearby: Santa Barbara Harbor, Leadbetter Beach, Stearns Wharf, and the Santa Barbara Yacht Club (,)

attractions.name: Santa Barbara Museum of Natural History Sea Center
attractions.description: The Sea Center on Stearns Wharf is a highly interactive regional aquarium and sister campus to the Santa Barbara Museum of Natural History. It offers visitors close encounters with marine animals from the Santa Barbara Channel through hands-on exhibits, a shark touch pool, and a theater. Guests can explore various marine habitats and work like scientists to discover the wonders of ocean life
attractions.hours: Monday–Sunday: 10:00 AM–5:00 PM
attractions.admission: Members: Free, Adult (18–64): $17, Senior (65+): $15, Teen (13–17): $15, Child (2–12): $13, Child (under 2): Free, SNAP/Cal Fresh (with EBT card and ID): $5
highlights: Live shark touch pool where visitors can gently pet small sharks and rays
Dive In: Our Changing Channel exhibit featuring moon jellies and a California Moray Eel
Interactive 1,500-gallon tidepool tank that children can crawl through for a unique perspective
Marine Mammal Mezzanine with a life-sized model of a Gray Whale mother and calf
BioLab where visitors can learn about the life cycles of marine animals like jellies
attractions.tips: Parking on Stearns Wharf is free for the first 90 minutes. Visitors typically spend 30 to 60 minutes exploring the exhibits. It is recommended to wear flat shoes when walking on the wharf's wooden planks to avoid getting heels stuck
attractions.nearby: Stearns Wharf, MOXI The Wolf Museum of Exploration + Innovation, Santa Barbara Maritime Museum, Deep Sea Wine Tasting Room, Cabrillo Bike Path

attractions.name: Santa Barbara Sailing Center
attractions.description: The Santa Barbara Sailing Center is a premier on-the-water destination that has been introducing visitors to the lifestyle and sport of sailing since 1967. Located in the Santa Barbara Harbor, it offers a diverse range of activities including public cruises aboard the Double Dolphin catamaran, private yacht charters, and ASA-certified sailing instruction. Guests can also enjoy self-guided adventures with kayak and stand-up paddleboard rentals to explore the local coastline and marine life
attractions.hours: Winter: Mon-Sun 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Summer: Mon-Sun 9:00 AM - 6:00 PM
attractions.admission: Prices vary by activity: Whale Watching ($54-$77.83, children free M-F), Sunset Cruises ($50-$77.83), Coastal Cruises ($40-$65.90), Kayak Rentals ($20-$30 for 2 hours), SUP Rentals ($25 for 2 hours), Sailing Lessons ($412-$702)
highlights: Public cruises aboard the 50-foot Double Dolphin catamaran
ASA-certified sailing instruction and certification courses
Kayak and stand-up paddleboard (SUP) rentals for harbor exploration
Seasonal whale watching excursions
Private yacht charters for special events and coastal getaways
Eco-friendly 'Green Business' certified practices and environmental commitment
attractions.tips: Dress in layers as it is cooler on the water, wear non-marking soled shoes, and arrive 20-30 minutes early for check-in. Reservations are required for all public cruises, and be sure to bring sunblock and a camera for wildlife sightings
attractions.nearby: Santa Barbara Harbor, Stearns Wharf, West Beach, Shoreline Park, Santa Barbara Maritime Museum

attractions.name: Santa Barbara Zoo
attractions.description: Spanning 30 acres near the Pacific Ocean, the Santa Barbara Zoo is home to more than 500 animals representing over 146 species. Known as one of the best small zoos in the United States, it features lush botanical gardens and innovative exhibits like the Australian Walkabout and the African Veldt. The zoo is dedicated to the preservation, conservation, and enhancement of the natural world through education and research
attractions.hours: Daily 9:30 AM – 7:00 PM (Spring/Summer, last entry 6:00 PM); 9:30 AM – 5:00 PM (Fall/Winter, last entry 4:00 PM)
attractions.admission: Online: Adults (13+) $27.95–$37.95, Children (2–12) $27.95, Children under 2 Free. Gate prices are typically $2 higher. Parking is $11 per vehicle. EBT cardholders $3 (up to 4 people)
highlights: Australian Walkabout
African Plains
Cats of Africa
The Forest's Edge
California Trails
Zoo Train
attractions.tips: Reservations are highly recommended and save $2 per ticket when purchased online. Hand-feeding the Masai giraffes is a popular experience available for an additional fee. Note that gorillas go off-habitat at 4:30 PM, so visit them earlier in the day. The zoo is very stroller-friendly, with single and double strollers available for rent at the gift store
attractions.nearby: Andree Clark Bird Refuge, East Beach, Stearns Wharf, Santa Barbara Cemetery, Chase Palm Park

attractions.name: Shoreline Park
attractions.description: Shoreline Park is a stunning 14.6-acre bluff-top park offering panoramic views of the Pacific Ocean, the Channel Islands, and the Santa Barbara coastline. This linear park features a paved one-mile walking path, expansive grassy areas, and a children's playground. Visitors can enjoy whale watching from the designated area or descend a wooden staircase to explore the tidal beach and tide pools below. (,)
attractions.address: Shoreline Dr and Santa Rosa Pl, Santa Barbara, CA 93109
attractions.hours: Monday: Sunrise - 10:00 PM; Tuesday: Sunrise - 10:00 PM; Wednesday: Sunrise - 10:00 PM; Thursday: Sunrise - 10:00 PM; Friday: Sunrise - 10:00 PM; Saturday: Sunrise - 10:00 PM; Sunday: Sunrise - 10:00 PM ()
highlights: Panoramic vistas of the Pacific Ocean and Channel Islands ()
Paved one-mile round-trip walking and jogging path ()
Children's playground designed for ages two to five ()
Whale watching area with a telescope and concrete whale tail statue ()
Wooden staircase providing access to a secluded tidal beach ()
Reservable picnic areas with tables and BBQ grills ()
attractions.tips: Check the tide tables before visiting the beach below the bluffs, as it is often completely submerged during high tide. The park is an excellent spot for seasonal whale watching, particularly for Gray Whales during their migration months. Parking is available in two small lots at the east and west ends of the park, though they can fill up quickly on weekends. Dogs are welcome but must remain on a leash at all times within the park. (,)
attractions.nearby: Leadbetter Beach, Stearns Wharf, Douglas Family Preserve, 1000 Steps Beach, Santa Barbara City College (,)

attractions.name: Stearns Wharf
attractions.description: Stearns Wharf is California's oldest working wooden wharf, dating back to 1872. Located at the end of State Street, it serves as a premier tourist destination offering a unique blend of restaurants, specialty shops, and educational attractions like the Sea Center. Visitors can enjoy panoramic views of the Pacific Ocean, the Santa Barbara coastline, and the Santa Ynez Mountains while strolling along its historic planks
attractions.hours: Kiosk opens at 07:00 AM, Kiosk closes at 10:00 PM
highlights: Oldest working wooden wharf in California, built in 1872
Home to the Santa Barbara Museum of Natural History Sea Center
Diverse dining options including the Santa Barbara Shellfish Company and Moby Dick Restaurant
Features unique specialty shops, wine tasting, and a bait & tackle shop
Famous Dolphin Fountain at the entrance sculpted by Bud Bottoms
Stunning views of the harbor, mountains, and offshore islands
attractions.tips: Arrive early to take advantage of the first 90 minutes of free parking directly on the wharf. Wear comfortable walking shoes as it is an old wooden structure with limited space. Dogs are allowed but must follow Waterfront animal regulations; some restaurants offer outdoor pet-friendly seating
attractions.nearby: Santa Barbara Harbor, West Beach, State Street Promenade, Santa Barbara Zoo, MOXI Science Museum

attractions.name: The Lark
attractions.description: Named after the Southern Pacific Railroad's sleek overnight Pullman train, The Lark is a premier shared-dining destination in the heart of Santa Barbara's vibrant Funk Zone. The restaurant features a locally-driven menu showcasing seasonal ingredients from the Central Coast, served family-style in a rustic yet sophisticated urban space. The setting, located in the historic Santa Barbara Fish Market building, integrates vintage and repurposed materials to reflect the neighborhood's industrial past
attractions.hours: Dinner: Sunday-Thursday 5:00 PM - 9:00 PM, Friday-Saturday 5:00 PM - 10:00 PM; Brunch: Saturday-Sunday 10:30 AM - 2:00 PM
highlights: Family-style shared dining experience celebrating Central Coast bounty
Located in the historic Santa Barbara Fish Market building in the Funk Zone
Menus feature locally sourced and responsibly grown seasonal ingredients
Rustic-urban design with original lighting, handmade furniture, and a 24-seat live-edge communal table
Full bar offering classic and modern craft cocktails and local Santa Barbara County wines
Awarded recognition from the MICHELIN Guide and Wine Spectator
attractions.tips: Reservations are highly recommended due to the restaurant's popularity. The crispy Brussels sprouts are a signature "must-try" dish. For a more energetic atmosphere, request seating at the bar or the communal table. Note that the menu is designed for sharing, so it's best to order several plates for the table
attractions.nearby: Lucky Penny, Santa Barbara Wine Collective, Loquita Santa Barbara, Riverbench Santa Barbara Tasting Room, Stearns Wharf, Reagan Ranch Center

attractions.name: West Beach
attractions.description: West Beach is a picturesque sandy shoreline nestled between Stearns Wharf and the Santa Barbara Harbor. It is a popular spot for sunbathing, swimming, and beach volleyball, featuring a wide palm-lined walkway and bike path. The beach also serves as a primary site for major community events, including the city's annual Fourth of July fireworks celebration (,)
attractions.hours: Daily: Sunrise – 10:00 PM ()
highlights: Numerous sand volleyball courts for public use ()
Kayaking and stand-up paddleboard rentals available on the sand ()
Direct access to the scenic Cabrillo Waterfront Bike Path ()
Prime location for the City of Santa Barbara's 4th of July fireworks display ())
Adjacent to the historic Stearns Wharf and Santa Barbara Harbor ()
attractions.tips: Note that dogs are strictly prohibited on the sand at West Beach. For a more tranquil experience, visit on weekday mornings to avoid the larger weekend crowds. Paid public parking is available in nearby city lots. Visitors can also download free audio tour files from the City's website to learn about the coastal trail's history (,)

attractions.name: La Super-Rica Taqueria
attractions.description: La Super-Rica Taqueria is a legendary, no-frills Mexican institution in Santa Barbara, famously known as a favorite of the late chef Julia Child. Housed in a humble turquoise-trimmed white shack, it is celebrated for its authentic home-style cooking and fresh corn tortillas made by hand in front of customers. The restaurant consistently draws long lines of locals and tourists eager to sample its small but flavorful menu of tacos, tamales, and specialty dishes. (,)
attractions.address: 622 N Milpas St, Santa Barbara, CA 93103
attractions.hours: Monday: 11:00 AM - 9:00 PM; Tuesday: Closed; Wednesday: Closed; Thursday: 11:00 AM - 9:00 PM; Friday: 11:00 AM - 9:30 PM; Saturday: 11:00 AM - 9:30 PM; Sunday: 11:00 AM - 9:00 PM (,)
highlights: Handmade corn tortillas prepared to order on a traditional grill
Famed as Julia Child's favorite Mexican restaurant in Santa Barbara
Signature dish: Super-Rica Especial
Classic chalkboard menu featuring tacos, tamales, and daily specials
Casual outdoor patio seating under a fiberglass roof
Authentic flavors founded by Jalisco native Isidoro Gonzalez in 1980
attractions.tips: This establishment is cash only, so come prepared or visit an ATM nearby. Lines often form before opening and can last over an hour; arrive early or visit during off-peak mid-afternoon hours to minimize wait times. Don't miss the daily specials listed on the blackboard, particularly the tamal of the day and the refreshing aguas frescas. (,)
attractions.nearby: Los Agaves, Santa Barbara County Bowl, East Beach, Chase Palm Park, Stearns Wharf ()

attractions.name: Los Agaves Mexican Restaurant
attractions.description: Los Agaves is an award-winning Mexican restaurant that has been family-owned and operated since 2008. It is renowned for its inventive dishes, high-quality fresh ingredients, and signature seafood specialties like halibut and shrimp enchiladas. The atmosphere blends casual counter-service with a sophisticated, mission-style dining environment
attractions.address: 600 N Milpas St, Santa Barbara, CA 93101
attractions.hours: Monday - Sunday: 11:00 AM - 9:00 PM
highlights: Extensive, unlimited self-service salsa bar featuring a variety of homemade salsas
Signature Los Agaves Enchiladas stuffed with fresh halibut and shrimp
Popular Agaves Burrito made with shrimp, Oaxaca cheese, and smoky chipotle sauce
Consistently voted "Best Mexican Restaurant" and "Best Salsa" in Santa Barbara
Upscale presentation of traditional Mexican cuisine in a family-friendly setting
attractions.tips: This is a counter-service restaurant where you order at the register before finding a table. It is highly recommended to arrive outside of peak lunch and dinner hours as the line often extends out the door. Don't miss the salsa bar, and be sure to try the house specialty seafood dishes which are locally famous
attractions.nearby: Santa Barbara Bowl, East Beach, The Funk Zone, Old Mission Santa Barbara

attractions.name: Santa Barbara Bowl
attractions.description: The Santa Barbara Bowl is an iconic 4,562-seat outdoor amphitheater nestled in a natural hillside setting with views of the Pacific Ocean. Built in 1936 as part of the WPA, the venue features a distinctive copper-roofed stage pavilion and hosts approximately 27 major concerts annually. It is renowned for its intimate atmosphere, historic stonework, and commitment to environmental sustainability
attractions.address: 1122 N Milpas St, Santa Barbara, CA 93103
attractions.hours: Box Office: Monday–Friday 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM; Performance Days: Opens at Noon; Concerts typically end by 10:00 PM
attractions.days_open: Seasonally from April through November; Box Office open Monday–Friday; Venue open on concert dates
attractions.admission: Prices vary by event; typically ranges from approximately $45.50 to $150+ depending on the artist and seating section
highlights: Historic 1936 amphitheater with a copper-roofed stage pavilion
Scranton Overlook providing panoramic ocean and coastline views
Greening the Bowl initiative targeting carbon-neutral operations
Intimate 4,562-seat capacity ensuring clear sightlines from all sections
Diverse on-site dining including Towbes Family Café and Parker Family BBQ
attractions.tips: Arrive at least 90 minutes early to navigate the hillside walk; bring layers as temperatures drop quickly after sunset; take advantage of the free bicycle valet; and note the strict "no bags" policy (purses must be 10" x 7" x 2" or smaller)
attractions.nearby: Santa Barbara High School, Milpas Street "Taco Trail" (including La Super-Rica Taqueria), Franceschi Park, Old Mission Santa Barbara, State Street Promenade

attractions.name: Telegraph Brewing Company
attractions.description: Telegraph Brewing Company was Santa Barbara's first packaging craft brewery, specializing in Belgian-inspired and uniquely American ales brewed with local ingredients. Founded in 2006 and later acquired by Epic Brewing, it became a cornerstone of the local beer scene known for its traditional techniques and innovative sour program. The brewery operated out of an industrial Quonset hut on the city's Eastside until its closure
attractions.address: 418 N Salsipuedes St, Santa Barbara, CA 93103-3127
attractions.hours: Tuesday: 3:00 PM - 9:00 PM, Wednesday: 3:00 PM - 9:00 PM, Thursday: 3:00 PM - 9:00 PM, Friday: 2:00 PM - 10:00 PM, Saturday: 2:00 PM - 10:00 PM, Sunday: 1:00 PM - 7:00 PM, Monday: Closed
highlights: Santa Barbara's first packaging craft brewery, established in 2006
Award-winning beers with multiple Great American Beer Festival and World Beer Cup medals
Specialized "Obscura" barrel-aging and award-winning sour beer program
Commitment to using 100% domestic and locally sourced agricultural ingredients
Dog-friendly and family-friendly industrial taproom atmosphere
Famous flagship brews like the California Ale and "What Hath God Wrought"
attractions.tips: While listed as closed in some recent 2025/2026 updates as the space transitioned to Cutler's Public House, travelers have historically recommended trying the "What Hath God Wrought" ale and noting that the taproom can get warm in the afternoon due to its glass-front design. Parking is limited on Salsipuedes Street, so expect to circle the block or walk from nearby
attractions.nearby: Carr Winery, Los Agaves, Third Window Brewing Co., The Funk Zone

attractions.name: Butterfly Beach
attractions.description: Butterfly Beach is a pristine coastal stretch located in the upscale community of Montecito, known for its unique east-west orientation that offers views of both sunrise and sunset over the Pacific. A favorite among locals, it provides an elegant yet laid-back atmosphere for sunbathing, swimming, surfing, and scenic walks. The beach is bordered by a beautiful seawall and a popular bike path that connects it to downtown Santa Barbara
attractions.hours: Monday: Open 24 hours, Tuesday: Open 24 hours, Wednesday: Open 24 hours, Thursday: Open 24 hours, Friday: Open 24 hours, Saturday: Open 24 hours, Sunday: Open 24 hours
highlights: Spectacular west-facing views ideal for watching both sunrises and sunsets
Pristine sandy shoreline perfect for sunbathing, swimming, and relaxing
Dog-friendly environment where pets are permitted on-leash
Excellent opportunities for surfing, kayaking, and stand-up paddleboarding
Direct access to the scenic Cabrillo Bike Path for cycling and walking
Frequent sightings of local marine life including dolphins and sea lions
attractions.tips: Parking is limited and fills up quickly on weekends, so arriving by bike or walking is recommended. The beach has no public restrooms or facilities, though dining options are nearby on Coast Village Road. Visitors should be mindful of the tide schedule as the beach can almost disappear at high tide, and watch out for natural tar on the sand
attractions.nearby: Four Seasons Resort The Biltmore Santa Barbara, Lotusland (Ganna Walska Lotusland), Coast Village Road shopping and dining, Santa Barbara Cemetery, and the Andree Clark Bird Refuge

attractions.name: Cold Spring Trail
attractions.description: Cold Spring Trail is a popular hiking destination in the Montecito foothills, known for its scenic pools, perennial streams, and lush sandstone canyons. The trail offers various routes, including a moderate loop and more challenging climbs to Montecito Peak or Tangerine Falls, featuring stunning views of the Santa Barbara coastline and Channel Islands
attractions.address: Cold Spring Trailhead, Santa Barbara, CA 93108
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours
highlights: Perennial streams and deep pools
Scenic views of Santa Barbara and the Channel Islands
Access to Tangerine Falls
Shaded canyon sections with Matilija sandstone
Dog-friendly
Connections to Montecito Peak and the Hot Springs Trail
attractions.tips: Watch for poison oak along the trail. Parking is limited on East Mountain Drive and fills up quickly on weekends, so arrive early to secure a spot. Wear shoes with good tread for creek crossings and rocky sections
attractions.nearby: Gould Park, Tangerine Falls, Montecito Peak, Hot Springs Trail, San Ysidro Trail

attractions.name: Ganna Walska Lotusland
attractions.description: Ganna Walska Lotusland is a world-renowned 37-acre botanical wonder and historic estate located in the foothills of Montecito. It features an extraordinary collection of over 3,000 plant species from around the world, arranged in more than 20 unique themed gardens. The estate was the personal vision of the eccentric opera singer and socialite Madame Ganna Walska, who spent over 40 years creating its whimsical and dramatic landscapes (,)
attractions.address: 695 Ashley Road (Administrative Office); Visitor Entrance is on Cold Springs Road near Sycamore Canyon Road ()
attractions.hours: Tours (Self-Guided and Docent-Led) are offered Wednesday through Saturday at 9:30 a.m. and 1:30 p.m. Reservations are required and timed for 2 hours ()
attractions.admission: Adults: $70; Youth (ages 3–12): $35; Children (2 & under): Free; Museums for All: $5; Docent-led tour: $15 additional ()
highlights: Over 20 distinct themed gardens, including the Japanese Garden, Blue Garden, and Cactus Garden ()
One of the world’s most extensive collections of rare and endangered cycads ()
The iconic Lotus Pond, which features stunning blooms during the summer months ()
Whimsical landscape features such as the 25-foot diameter working Zodiac Clock and topiary garden ()
Historic Mediterranean-style mansion designed by architect Reginald Johnson ()
attractions.tips: Advance reservations are mandatory and should be made 3–4 weeks in advance as same-day bookings are not available. Arrive exactly 15 minutes before your scheduled time, as late arrivals may not be permitted entry. No food or picnicking is allowed on the property, and pets are not permitted (except service animals). Wear comfortable walking shoes, as the tour involves approximately 2 miles of walking across varied terrain (,)
attractions.nearby: Santa Barbara Botanic Garden, Casa del Herrero, Old Mission Santa Barbara, Alice Keck Park Memorial Garden, Stearns Wharf (,)

attractions.name: Hot Springs Canyon
attractions.description: A scenic and moderately challenging 2.5 to 3-mile round-trip hike in the Santa Ynez Mountains that leads to natural geothermal pools and historic ruins of an 1800s resort. The trail offers panoramic ocean and mountain views while winding through shaded oak and sycamore canyons
attractions.hours: Sunrise to sunset daily
attractions.admission: Free (Note: Parking at the trailhead requires a National Forest Adventure Pass or America the Beautiful Pass)
highlights: Natural mineral-rich geothermal pools with cascading rock-walled soaking areas
Historic stone ruins and exotic plantings from the original 19th-century hot springs resort
Panoramic ocean views of the Santa Barbara coastline and Channel Islands
Lush riparian habitat featuring oak and sycamore trees alongside Hot Springs Creek
Picturesque Hot Springs Falls waterfall located along the trail
Managed conservation area spanning 462 acres of protected open space
attractions.tips: Parking at the trailhead is extremely limited (max 10 vehicles) and strictly enforced; arrive before 7 AM or use a rideshare service to avoid towing. Wear sturdy hiking shoes for steep, rocky sections and bring plenty of water as there are no facilities on-site. Respect private property and stay on marked trails as the trailhead is located in a residential neighborhood
attractions.nearby: Cold Spring Trail, San Ysidro Trail, Lotusland, Casa del Herrero, Butterfly Beach, Manning Park

attractions.name: Montecito
attractions.description: Montecito is an effortlessly chic coastal enclave known for its luxury, natural beauty, and concentration of wealthy residents. Nestled between the Santa Ynez Mountains and the Pacific Ocean, it offers a blend of stunning coastline, lush botanical gardens, and upscale social hubs. This world-renowned celebrity retreat maintains a semi-rural, private atmosphere while being just minutes from downtown Santa Barbara (,)
attractions.address: 1469 E. Valley Road, Santa Barbara, CA 93108 ()
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours daily ()
highlights: Lotusland: A world-renowned 37-acre botanical garden with rare and exotic plant collections
Butterfly Beach: A pristine, west-facing beach perfect for sunset watching and coastal walks
Coast Village Road: The community's "Main Street," featuring luxury boutiques and gourmet dining
Casa del Herrero: A National Historic Landmark showcasing impeccable Spanish Colonial Revival architecture
Scenic Hiking Trails: A network of paths like Cold Spring and San Ysidro offering mountain and ocean views
San Ysidro Ranch: A storied luxury resort and historic landmark nestled in the foothills
attractions.tips: Reservations are required for tours of Lotusland and Casa del Herrero. Parking at Butterfly Beach is limited, so arrive early or on weekdays. Explore both Coast Village Road and the Upper Village for a complete shopping and dining experience. Bring water and sturdy shoes for mountain trails (,)
attractions.nearby: Santa Barbara Zoo, Stearns Wharf, The Funk Zone, Old Mission Santa Barbara, Summerland ()

attractions.name: Chumash Heritage National Marine Sanctuary
attractions.description: The Chumash Heritage National Marine Sanctuary is a 4,543-square-mile protected area encompassing 116 miles of Central California's coastline from San Luis Obispo to the Gaviota Coast. It is the first tribally-nominated sanctuary in the United States, designed to protect nationally significant natural, cultural, and historical resources through collaborative co-stewardship between NOAA and Indigenous groups. (,)
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours daily ()
highlights: First tribally-nominated national marine sanctuary in the U.S. ()
Protects submerged Chumash sacred sites and ancient village locations ()
Home to diverse marine life including blue whales, sea otters, and leatherback sea turtles ()
Includes over 200 documented shipwrecks, such as the Gold Rush-era SS Yankee Blade ()
Features unique geological formations like the Rodriguez Seamount and Santa Lucia Bank ()
attractions.tips: Respect the cultural and spiritual significance of the area, especially near Point Conception; follow vessel speed guidelines and discharge regulations; and enjoy whale watching from shore-side trails like the Ontario Ridge Loop (,)
attractions.nearby: Gaviota State Park, Jalama Beach, Channel Islands National Marine Sanctuary, Pismo Beach (,)

attractions.name: Coal Oil Point Natural Reserve
attractions.description: Coal Oil Point Reserve is a 170-acre coastal reserve part of the University of California Natural Reserve System, protecting a variety of rare habitats including salt marshes, dunes, and vernal pools. It serves as a vital research and education site while offering public access to its beautiful shoreline and trails. The reserve is notably a significant breeding ground for the threatened western snowy plover ()
attractions.hours: Monday: Sunrise to Sunset; Tuesday: Sunrise to Sunset; Wednesday: Sunrise to Sunset; Thursday: Sunrise to Sunset; Friday: Sunrise to Sunset; Saturday: Sunrise to Sunset; Sunday: Sunrise to Sunset (Day use only; Nature Center open first Saturday of month 9:00 AM – 1:00 PM) (,)
attractions.admission: Free for self-guided individuals; $20/person suggested donation for guided tours ()
highlights: Devereux Slough, a top birding site for migratory species ()
Sands Beach, known for its pristine coastline and surfing ()
Western Snowy Plover nesting sites and docent-led viewing ()
Two-mile loop interpretive trail through eight distinct habitats ()
Nature Center featuring research exhibits and local natural history ()
Rocky intertidal zones and tide pools rich with marine life ()
attractions.tips: Parking is extremely limited; visitors are encouraged to park in Isla Vista (near Camino Majorca) and walk or use the UCSB paid parking lots. Dogs, drones, and smoking are strictly prohibited to protect sensitive wildlife. Be mindful of the snowy plover nesting areas and stay on designated trails or the wet sand (,)
attractions.nearby: University of California, Santa Barbara (UCSB), Goleta Butterfly Grove, Ellwood Mesa Open Space, Isla Vista ()

attractions.name: El Capitán State Beach
attractions.description: El Capitán State Beach features a beautiful sandy shoreline, rocky tide pools, and groves of sycamore and oak trees along El Capitán Creek. It is a popular destination for swimming, fishing, surfing, picnicking, and camping on the scenic Santa Barbara coast. (,)
attractions.hours: Monday: 8 am to Sunset; Tuesday: 8 am to Sunset; Wednesday: 8 am to Sunset; Thursday: 8 am to Sunset; Friday: 8 am to Sunset; Saturday: 8 am to Sunset; Sunday: 8 am to Sunset (Note: Park may be subject to temporary closures for construction) ()
attractions.admission: Vehicle Day Use: $10.00, Hourly Parking: $3.00, Large Bus: $100.00, Small Bus: $50.00, Hike and Bike Camping: $10.00 per person per night (,)
highlights: Sandy beach and rocky tide pools ()
132 standard campsites and group camping areas ()
Sycamore and oak groves along El Capitán Creek ()
Swimming, surfing, and fishing access ()
Coastal hiking and walking paths ()
Seasonal lifeguard towers from Memorial Day to Labor Day ()
attractions.tips: Be prepared for natural tar on the beach; baby oil or wipes can help remove it from feet. Ensure you take Exit 117 for El Capitan State Beach off Highway 101, as the El Capitan Ranch exit leads elsewhere. Dogs are allowed in the campground and day-use areas on a leash but are prohibited on the beach. A day-use pass is also valid for entry to Refugio and Gaviota State Beaches on the same day. (,)
attractions.nearby: Refugio State Beach, Gaviota State Beach, El Capitan Canyon, Arroyo Hondo Reserve (,)

attractions.name: Gaviota Hot Springs ()
attractions.description: Gaviota Hot Springs, also known as Las Cruces Hot Springs, is a rustic geothermal feature located within Gaviota State Park. The springs consist of two main pools known for their milky blue, sulfur-rich waters that offer therapeutic benefits for the skin and muscles. Surrounded by lush, jungle-like vegetation, the site provides a serene and natural soaking experience accessible via a short hike along the Gaviota Peak trail. (,)
attractions.address: Gaviota Park Boundary Road, Goleta, CA 93117 (,)
attractions.hours: 7:00 a.m. to Sunset daily ()
attractions.admission: Free ($2 parking fee required at the trailhead) (,)
highlights: Milky blue, mineral-rich sulfur water with therapeutic properties ()
Two main soaking pools, with the upper pool being warmer and clearer ()
Lush, jungle-like surrounding vegetation providing shade and seclusion ()
Accessible via a short 0.5 to 1-mile hike with scenic mountain views ()
Historical rock-and-concrete basins dating back to the 1930s New Deal era ()
Proximity to other major hiking trails like Gaviota Peak and Wind Caves ()
attractions.tips: Arrive early in the morning or late afternoon to avoid crowds, as the pools are small and can only accommodate about 6-7 people each. Bring $2 in cash for the self-registration parking fee at the trailhead. Wear sturdy, trail-appropriate footwear as the path is often rocky, uneven, and can be muddy. Be prepared for a strong sulfur smell, which is natural to the mineral waters. (,)
attractions.nearby: Gaviota Peak, Gaviota Wind Caves, Gaviota State Beach, Nojoqui Falls, and Gaviota Pass Overlook ()

attractions.name: Goleta Beach Park ()
attractions.description: Goleta Beach Park is a popular 29-acre coastal destination known for its 1,500-foot fishing pier and extensive sunbathing beaches (). The park offers a variety of recreational facilities, including group picnic areas, volleyball courts, and access to paved biking trails (). It also serves as a gateway to the Goleta Slough, a haven for birdwatching and nature enthusiasts ()
attractions.hours: 8:00 AM to Sunset (Daily) ()
attractions.admission: Free (Parking and park entry are free) ()
highlights: 1,500-foot public fishing pier ()
Group picnic areas with BBQ grills and pavilions ()
Paved biking trails connecting to the Obern Trail ()
Free beach wheelchair program ()
Birdwatching at the adjacent Goleta Slough wetlands ()
On-site kayak and stand-up paddleboard rentals ()
attractions.tips: No fishing license is required to fish from the public pier (). Beach wheelchairs should be reserved in advance through the Parks Call Center, especially during the peak season from Memorial Day to Labor Day (). Be mindful of natural tar on the beach and consider wearing old shoes for walking ()
attractions.nearby: University of California, Santa Barbara (UCSB) (), Santa Barbara Airport (), South Coast Railroad Museum (), Rancho La Patera & Stow House ()

attractions.name: Goleta Butterfly Grove at Ellwood Mesa
attractions.description: The Goleta Butterfly Grove at Ellwood Mesa is a spectacular 230-acre coastal open space that serves as a primary overwintering site for thousands of migratory monarch butterflies. Visitors can walk through scenic eucalyptus groves, enjoy sweeping ocean views from the coastal bluffs, and access the pristine Ellwood Beach. The preserve is a vital habitat protected by the City of Goleta and offers a peaceful natural escape for hikers and wildlife enthusiasts
attractions.address: 7729 Hollister Ave (Sperling Preserve parking lot), Goleta, CA 93117; secondary access via Coronado Drive
attractions.hours:
Monday: Sunrise - Sunset
Tuesday: Sunrise - Sunset
Wednesday: Sunrise - Sunset
Thursday: Sunrise - Sunset
Friday: Sunrise - Sunset
Saturday: Sunrise - Sunset
Sunday: Sunrise - Sunset
attractions.days_open: Open Daily (Year-round, with peak butterfly viewing Nov-Feb)
attractions.admission: Free (Donations are accepted for the docent program)
attractions.phone: (805) 961-7500 (City of Goleta Main Line) or (805) 562-5509 (Parks & Open Space Division)
highlights: Overwintering monarch butterfly clusters
Scenic hiking trails across the 230-acre Ellwood Mesa
Access to the uncrowded Ellwood Beach via coastal bluff trails
Docent-led tours available on weekends during peak season
Spectacular ocean views and sunset vantage points from the bluffs
Dog-friendly on-leash trails throughout the preserve
attractions.tips: Visit between 11:00 AM and 2:00 PM on sunny days to see butterflies in flight; they cluster for warmth when it is cool. Wear sturdy closed-toe shoes for varied trail terrain and bring binoculars for better viewing of high clusters. Note that the main parking lot is across from Ellwood Elementary School on Hollister Avenue, and a portable restroom is located there
attractions.nearby: Coronado Butterfly Preserve, Sandpiper Golf Club, Bacara Resort (The Ritz-Carlton Bacara), Santa Barbara Shores Park, and Lake Los Carneros Park

attractions.name: Refugio State Beach
attractions.description: Refugio State Beach is a picturesque paradise located about 22 miles west of Santa Barbara, known for its distinct palm trees near Refugio Creek and breathtaking sunsets. The beach offers excellent opportunities for coastal fishing, swimming, picnicking, and kayaking in a tropical island-like setting
attractions.hours: Monday - Sunday: 8:00 AM - Sunset
attractions.admission: Vehicle Day Use: $10.00; Seniors (62+): $9.00; Disabled vehicle with state discount pass: $5.00
highlights: Distinct palm-lined shores and a tropical island feel
Excellent coastal fishing, swimming, and kayaking opportunities
67 standard campsites and 3 group sites for overnight stays
Beach wheelchairs available for public use at no cost
Year-round lifeguard patrols with staffed towers during summer months
Scenic picnic areas and hiking trails with ocean views
attractions.tips: Dogs are allowed in the campground and day-use areas on a 6-foot leash but are strictly prohibited on the beach itself. Campsite reservations should be made well in advance through ReserveCalifornia.com as the park frequently fills up, especially on weekends
attractions.nearby: El Capitan State Beach, Gaviota State Park, Nojoqui Falls Park & Trail

attractions.name: UC Santa Barbara Visitor Center
attractions.description: The UC Santa Barbara Visitor Center offers guided and self-guided tours of its stunning 1,000-acre oceanfront campus. These tours provide prospective students and families with a comprehensive overview of academic life, campus facilities, and the university's unique coastal environment. It is the official starting point for exploring the university's vibrant community and world-class research infrastructure. (,)
attractions.address: 1102 Student Affairs Administrative Services Building (SAASB), Santa Barbara, CA 93106-2014
attractions.hours: Monday: 9:00 AM – 4:00 PM; Tuesday: 9:00 AM – 4:00 PM; Wednesday: 9:00 AM – 4:00 PM; Thursday: 9:00 AM – 4:00 PM; Friday: 9:00 AM – 4:00 PM; Saturday: Closed (Open 10:00 AM – 2:00 PM on select Saturdays in April and May); Sunday: Closed ()
highlights: Stunning ocean views from Campus Point and the UCSB Lagoon
The iconic 190-foot Storke Tower, a campus landmark
Davidson Library, featuring extensive collections and study spaces
The University Center (UCen), a hub for dining and student services
Proximity to the lively student community of Isla Vista
State-of-the-art research facilities and residence halls ()
attractions.tips: Visitors are strongly encouraged to register for guided tours in advance as they often reach capacity. For a flexible experience, the "UC Santa Barbara Guides" mobile app offers an interactive audio walking tour. Parking is recommended in Lot 16 or Parking Structure 18 (Mesa Structure), where permits can be purchased via the ParkMobile app or kiosks. (,)
attractions.nearby: Isla Vista, Goleta Beach Park, Coal Oil Point Reserve, Sandpiper Golf Club, Santa Barbara Airport ()

attractions.name: UCSB Arts & Lectures
attractions.description: Founded in 1959, UCSB Arts & Lectures is a premier performing arts and public lectures presenter that brings world-class cultural experiences to the Central Coast. It hosts over 100 events annually, including concerts, dance performances, and talks by influential thinkers and authors
attractions.address: Campbell Hall, University of California, Santa Barbara, Santa Barbara, CA 93106-5030
attractions.hours: Box Office: Mon-Fri 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Sat 12:00 PM - 4:00 PM (Oct-May, phone only)
attractions.admission: Varies by event; many are free for students or the public (e.g., Martín Espada was Free). Series subscriptions offer up to 25% discounts
highlights: Presents over 100 world-class events annually including music, dance, and theater
Hosts influential global leaders and Nobel Prize winners like Malala Yousafzai and Joe Biden
Operates extensive educational outreach through the 'Access for ALL' program
Features '¡Viva el Arte de Santa Barbara!' presenting free Latin American cultural events
Utilizes major venues like Campbell Hall, the Granada Theatre, and Arlington Theatre
attractions.tips: Student tickets require a valid ID. Arrive early for parking at campus venues as fees are required. Digital tickets are accessible via mobile devices
attractions.nearby: Goleta Beach Park, UC Santa Barbara Lagoon, South Coast Railroad Museum, Lake Los Carneros Park

attractions.name: Beckmen Vineyards
attractions.description: Beckmen Vineyards is a family-owned estate winery in the Santa Ynez Valley dedicated to crafting high-end Rhône wines from certified biodynamic grapes. The property features a rustic red barn winery, scenic tasting decks, and private gazebos overlooking tranquil duck ponds and rolling vineyards. It is renowned for being the first biodynamic vineyard in Santa Barbara County
attractions.hours: Monday - Sunday: 11:00 AM – 5:00 PM
attractions.admission: Wine Tasting: $30 for 5 premier wines (free for members); Farm to Glass Tour & Tasting: $60 per person; Private Tour & Wine Tasting: $125 per person; Bottle Service at Gazebo/Dock: Minimum one bottle purchase for every two people
highlights: First biodynamically farmed vineyard in Santa Barbara County
Specializes in estate-grown Rhône varietals such as Syrah and Grenache
Features scenic outdoor tasting decks and private gazebos by a duck pond
Grapes sourced from the Purisima Mountain Vineyard known for limestone-rich soil
Family and dog-friendly atmosphere for casual picnics and tastings
attractions.tips: Reservations are highly recommended for all experiences. For the Farm to Glass Tour, wear sturdy walking shoes as the terrain is uneven. Outside food is permitted only at private gazebo or dock locations with a bottle service reservation
attractions.nearby: Roblar Winery, Lincourt Vineyards, Buttonwood Farm Winery & Vineyard, Ostrichland USA, and Downtown Los Olivos tasting rooms

attractions.name: Brewer-Clifton
attractions.description: Founded in 1996, Brewer-Clifton produces world-class wines that reflect the geographic and climatic uniqueness of the Sta. Rita Hills appellation. Winemaker Greg Brewer focuses on crafting refined Chardonnay and Pinot Noir using neutral barrels and whole-cluster fermentation to highlight the purest voice of the vineyard
attractions.address: 2367 Alamo Pintado Ave, Los Olivos, CA 93441
attractions.hours: Monday: CLOSED, Tuesday: CLOSED, Wednesday: 10 AM–5 PM, Thursday: 10 AM–5 PM, Friday: 10 AM–5 PM, Saturday: 10 AM–5 PM, Sunday: 10 AM–5 PM
attractions.admission: Signature Sta. Rita Hills Tasting: $35 per person; Different Dialects Tasting: $25 per person; Complimentary for Members
highlights: Focus on 100% estate-grown Pinot Noir and Chardonnay from the Sta. Rita Hills AVA
Founded by acclaimed Winemaker of the Year Greg Brewer
Wines are raised in neutral cooperage with a focus on whole-cluster fermentation for Pinot Noir
Offers curated tasting flights including single-vineyard designates and diverse labels like Diatom and Ex Post Facto
Private, intimate winery tastings available at their Lompoc production facility by appointment
attractions.tips: Reservations are highly recommended to ensure a personalized experience, though walk-ins are welcome if space allows. For those visiting the Lompoc production site, note that there is a request for a six-bottle minimum purchase per household
attractions.nearby: Los Olivos tasting rooms and boutiques, Lompoc Wine Ghetto, La Purísima Mission State Historic Park, Melville Winery, Sanford Winery & Vineyards

attractions.name: Cachuma Lake Recreation Area
attractions.description: Cachuma Lake Recreation Area is a 3,100-acre reservoir nestled between the Santa Ynez and San Rafael Mountains, offering a tranquil escape for outdoor enthusiasts. Since it serves as a primary domestic water supply, body contact with the water is prohibited, but visitors can enjoy world-class fishing, boating, and guided wildlife cruises. The park features extensive camping facilities, including yurts and cabins, as well as miles of hiking trails and a nature center
attractions.hours: Day-Use: 6:00 AM to Sunset. Gate staffed: 7:00 AM to 5:00 PM (later on Summer Fridays). Office: 7:00 AM to 3:30 PM
attractions.admission: $10 per vehicle for Day Use. Additional fees for boats ($15), kayaks/canoes ($5), and camping ($30-$55+). Nature cruises: $17 adults, $7 kids
highlights: Freshwater fishing for bass, trout, and catfish
Guided wildlife cruises and nature walks
Cachuma Lake Disc Golf Course
Neal Taylor Nature Center with hands-on exhibits
Extensive camping options including cabins and yurts
Boating and kayaking
attractions.tips: Body contact with the lake (swimming, wading) is strictly prohibited as it is a domestic water reservoir. Reservations for lake cruises and premium camping sites (especially yurts) should be made well in advance. Fishing is excellent year-round, but trout are specifically stocked during winter months
attractions.nearby: Solvang (Danish village), Chumash Casino Resort, Cold Spring Tavern, Los Padres National Forest, Santa Ynez wineries

attractions.name: Chumash Casino Resort ()
attractions.description: Chumash Casino Resort is a premier entertainment destination in the heart of Santa Barbara's wine country, offering a world-class gaming floor, luxury accommodations, and fine dining. The resort's mission is to provide 'freedom from the everyday' through its AAA Four Diamond-rated hotel, top-tier spa, and non-stop gaming action. It serves as a central hub for visitors to the Santa Ynez Valley looking for a blend of excitement and relaxation. ()
attractions.hours: Casino: Open 24/7; The Pool: 8 AM – 8 PM Daily; The Spa: Fri-Tue 9:30 AM – 6:30 PM; Willows: Lounge 4 PM – 10 PM, Dinner 5 PM – 10 PM (,,)
attractions.admission: Casino admission is Free (must be 21+); Hotel and Spa services have varying rates; Concert tickets are sold per event (,)
highlights: 115,000 square foot gaming floor with 2,300+ slots and 45+ table games ()
AAA Four Diamond-rated hotel with 320 rooms and 58 suites ()
Rooftop pool deck with a 135-foot tower offering views of the Santa Ynez Valley ()
Willows, a Four Diamond-rated fine dining restaurant with a premier wine list ()
The Samala Showroom, hosting world-class live entertainment and concerts ()
Full-service spa and wellness center offering stress-relieving treatments ()
attractions.tips: All casino and hotel pool guests must be 21 or older with a valid government-issued photo ID. Join 'THE CLUB' for free to earn reward points on gaming, dining, and spa purchases, and to receive a new member offer of up to $500 in slot free play. Free parking and shuttle services are often available. (,)
attractions.nearby: Solvang (Danish-themed village), Santa Ynez Airport, Carr Winery Santa Ynez, Sunstone Winery, and the Santa Ynez Valley vineyards ()

attractions.name: Chumash Painted Cave State Historic Park
attractions.description: This 7.5-acre state park preserves a small sandstone cave containing some of the finest remaining rock art created by the Chumash Native Americans. The pictographs, created with mineral pigments, likely depict Chumash cosmology and religious ceremonies dating back several centuries
attractions.address: Painted Cave Road, Santa Barbara, CA 93105
attractions.hours: Monday: 12:00 AM - 11:59 PM, Tuesday: 12:00 AM - 11:59 PM, Wednesday: 12:00 AM - 11:59 PM, Thursday: 12:00 AM - 11:59 PM, Friday: 12:00 AM - 11:59 PM, Saturday: 12:00 AM - 11:59 PM, Sunday: 12:00 AM - 11:59 PM
highlights: Authentic Chumash pictographs in red, black, and white pigments
Preservation of centuries-old Chumash cosmology and religious symbols
Protective iron gate allowing safe viewing while preventing vandalism
Scenic drive up Painted Cave Road with panoramic coastal and mountain views
Listed on the National Register of Historic Places since 1972
attractions.tips: Bring a strong flashlight to better illuminate the art inside the dark cave. Flash photography is strictly prohibited to protect the pigments. The access road is extremely narrow and winding; trailers and RVs should not attempt the drive. Parking is limited to just one or two small roadside pullouts
attractions.nearby: Cold Spring Tavern, Knapp's Castle, Santa Barbara Botanic Garden, San Marcos Pass, Inspiration Point

attractions.name: Elverhøj Museum of History & Art
attractions.description: Elverhøj Museum of History & Art is a community museum located in a historic hand-crafted structure modeled after 18th-century Danish farmhouses. It is dedicated to documenting the history of Solvang, celebrating Danish culture and the Danish-American pioneer spirit, and promoting fine art through rotating exhibitions. The museum offers visitors a glimpse into the town's heritage through interactive displays, artifacts, and a tranquil backyard garden. (,)
attractions.hours: Thursday – Monday: 11:00 a.m. – 5:00 p.m.; Tuesday – Wednesday: Closed. Closed on Easter Sunday, 4th of July, Thanksgiving, Christmas Eve, Christmas Day, New Year’s Eve, and New Year’s Day. ()
attractions.admission: Adults and Children (12+): $5.00, Seniors (65+): $4.00, Children under 12: Free, Active Military/Veterans: Free, Museums for All: Free, NARM/ROAM Members: Free ()
highlights: Historic hand-crafted structure built in 18th-century Danish farmhouse style ()
Viking Exhibit featuring tools, replicas, and a hands-on rune alphabet activity ()
The Solvang Room with a large-scale diorama of the village as it appeared circa 1914 ()
Rotating art gallery showcasing regional, national, and international artists ()
Beautiful backyard garden area with a gazebo and a chime-equipped bell tower ()
Museum Store offering curated Danish and Scandinavian books, jewelry, and gifts ()
attractions.tips: The museum is located in a quiet residential area about a 10-minute walk from the main tourist center, making parking relatively easy. Plan to spend about 45 minutes to an hour to fully explore the exhibits and the garden. Don't miss the detailed interior of the miniature building in the Solvang Room. (,)
attractions.nearby: Little Mermaid Fountain, Hans Christian Andersen Museum, Old Mission Santa Inés, Solvang Vintage Motorcycle Museum, California Nature Art Museum ()

attractions.name: Fess Parker Winery & Vineyard
attractions.description: Fess Parker Winery is a family-owned estate established in 1989 by the legendary actor Fess Parker. Located along the Foxen Canyon Wine Trail, it is renowned for its high-quality Rhône and Burgundian varietals grown on its expansive 714-acre estate. The winery offers a relaxing and hospitable environment with scenic vineyard views and a focus on small-lot, vineyard-designated wines
attractions.address: 6200 Foxen Canyon Road, Los Olivos, CA 93441
attractions.hours: Monday-Sunday: 10:00 am – 5:00 pm
attractions.admission: Classic Tasting Flight: $25 (complimentary for Wine Club members and Fess Parker Inn guests); Group Tastings (10-40 people): $60 per person; Private Room Tasting: $75 per person; Addendum Tasting Experience: $60 per person; Rodney’s Vineyard Tour & Tasting: $800 for up to 8 people
highlights: Extensive 714-acre estate vineyard named Rodney’s Vineyard, renowned for Rhône varietals
Newly renovated modern tasting room and scenic veranda seating
Family and pet-friendly "Parker Family Oak Grove" picnic area
Featured prominently in the 2004 film 'Sideways' as the fictional Frass Canyon winery
Iconic golden coonskin cap logo on labels, a nod to Fess Parker's acting career
Winner of "Top 100 Winery" awards from Wine & Spirits Magazine in 2022 and 2023
attractions.tips: Reservations are strongly recommended for tastings, especially on weekends, though walk-ins are welcome for wine by the glass or bottle purchases. Outside food is permitted in the designated Oak Grove picnic area with the purchase of Fess Parker wine
attractions.nearby: Demetria Estate Winery, Koehler Winery, Andrew Murray Vineyards, Firestone Vineyard, and the town of Los Olivos

attractions.name: Firestone Walker Brewing Company - Buellton Taproom & Barrelworks
attractions.description: The Firestone Walker Buellton Taproom offers a spacious multi-level dining area with a gastropub menu specifically geared for beer pairing. It is home to Barrelworks, a dedicated wild ale cellar and 'cathedral of barrels' that specializes in eccentric creations made with locally foraged ingredients. Visitors can enjoy a wide array of award-winning craft beers alongside signature dishes in a friendly, high-energy atmosphere
attractions.hours: Sunday – Thursday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM; Friday – Saturday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM
highlights: Barrelworks Wild Ale Cellar and aging facility
Spacious multi-level gastropub with two outdoor patios
Extensive selection of Firestone Walker favorites and Barrelworks creations on tap
Weekly specials including Taco Tuesdays and Pizza & Pints Wednesdays
Dog-friendly outdoor seating area
Happy Hour specials Sunday through Friday from 3 PM to 6 PM
attractions.tips: The taproom operates on a first-come, first-served basis and does not accept reservations. Santa Ynez Valley locals receive a 25% discount on beer, food, and gear every Monday with a valid local ID. It is conveniently located right off the freeway and within walking distance of nearby hotels like the Marriott and Hilton
attractions.nearby: Solvang, OstrichLand USA, Mendenhall's Museum of Gasoline Pumps & Petroliana, Sideways Inn

attractions.name: Foxen Canyon Wine Trail
attractions.description: The Foxen Canyon Wine Trail represents a collection of 16 premier wineries and tasting rooms situated along a scenic 30-mile stretch of Foxen Canyon Road. This historic route winds through the rolling hills and oak-studded landscapes of Northern Santa Barbara County, connecting the charming town of Los Olivos with the Santa Maria Valley. Visitors can enjoy a diverse array of world-class varietals, including acclaimed Pinot Noir, Chardonnay, and Rhone-style wines, in settings ranging from rustic tasting "shacks" to elegant estate vineyards
attractions.hours: Most tasting rooms are open daily from 11:00 AM to 4:00 PM; some locations open as early as 10:00 AM or stay open until 5:00 PM
attractions.admission: Individual tasting fees typically range from $25 to $45; special event Passports (Summer and Christmas) offer multi-winery tastings for a set price
highlights: 30 miles of scenic country roads through rolling hills and vineyards
16 world-class wineries including Foxen Vineyard, Fess Parker, and Zaca Mesa
Home to diverse varietals like Pinot Noir, Syrah, and Chardonnay
Annual Summer and Christmas 'Passport' events with multi-winery access
Historic landmarks along the route, such as the San Ramon Chapel
Beautiful picnic grounds and estate tours available at many locations
attractions.tips: Reservations are highly recommended and often required for tastings at most wineries along the trail. It is a great idea to pack a picnic lunch to enjoy at the scenic outdoor tables at Rancho Sisquoc or Riverbench. Start your journey at one end (either Los Olivos or Santa Maria) to make the most of the 30-mile drive without backtracking
attractions.nearby: Los Olivos, Solvang, Santa Maria Valley, San Ramon Chapel, Los Olivos Grocery

attractions.name: Hans Christian Andersen Museum
attractions.description: The Hans Christian Andersen Museum is a charming space dedicated to the life and legacy of the legendary Danish author, located on the second floor of The Book Loft in Solvang. Operated by the non-profit Ugly Duckling Foundation, the museum features a private collection of first editions, original letters, and intricate paper cuttings created by Andersen himself. Visitors can explore detailed displays and miniature models that bring his most famous fairy tales, like "The Little Mermaid" and "The Princess and the Pea," to life
attractions.hours: Monday – Sunday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM
attractions.admission: Free (donations are welcome to support the non-profit Ugly Duckling Foundation)
highlights: Exhibits on Andersen’s early life and his unrequited love for singer Jenny Lind, the "Swedish Nightingale."
An extensive collection of first edition books and illustrated volumes of his fairy tales
Exquisitely handcrafted miniature models, including a replica of Andersen’s childhood home
Displays of intricate paper patterns fashioned by the author while narrating his stories
Features on Jean Hersholt, the Hollywood philanthropist who translated many of Andersen's works
Located in the historic Book Loft building, which also houses a specialized collection of Danish literature
attractions.tips: The museum is located upstairs and is only accessible by a staircase, which may be a consideration for those with mobility issues. While admission is free, it is recommended to support the local independent bookstore or leave a small donation. The museum is a compact space, making it a perfect quick cultural stop that can be thoroughly enjoyed in about 20-30 minutes
attractions.nearby: Solvang Park (home to a bust of Hans Christian Andersen), Old Mission Santa Inés, Elverhøj Museum of History & Art, Solvang Festival Theater, and Hans Christian Andersen Park

attractions.name: Industrial Eats
attractions.description: Industrial Eats is a quality-centered dining experience and craft butcher shop tucked away in a repurposed warehouse. The restaurant features two wood-fired ovens churning out a rotating menu of seasonal dishes sourced from sustainable farms. The space is known for its communal dining atmosphere and a distinctive produce-themed mural on its exterior wall
attractions.hours: Monday: 12:00 PM - 8:00 PM, Tuesday: 12:00 PM - 8:00 PM, Wednesday: 12:00 PM - 8:00 PM, Thursday: 12:00 PM - 8:00 PM, Friday: 12:00 PM - 8:00 PM, Saturday: 12:00 PM - 8:00 PM, Sunday: 12:00 PM - 8:00 PM
highlights: Two wood-fired ovens for seasonal, artisanal dishes
In-house craft butcher shop with house-made specialties
Communal dining experience at large shared wooden tables
Rotating menu presented on hanging kraft butcher paper
Sustainably sourced ingredients from local purveyors
Iconic exterior graffiti mural depicting produce and groceries
attractions.tips: The restaurant is located in an industrial park and can be difficult to find; look for the large, colorful mural on the exterior wall. Expect a counter-service format and communal seating that can get busy during peak hours
attractions.nearby: OstrichLand USA, Mendenhall's Museum of Gasoline Pumps & Petroliana, Figueroa Mountain Brewing Co., Santa Ynez Valley Botanic Garden

attractions.name: La Purísima Mission State Historic Park
attractions.description: La Purísima Mission State Historic Park is the most completely restored of the 21 Spanish missions in California, featuring ten original buildings and over 30 furnished rooms. Founded in 1787 on historic Chumash lands, the 1,928-acre park preserves the rich history of the mission era and the subsequent restoration by the Civilian Conservation Corps. Visitors can explore a working mission environment, including livestock, gardens, and an extensive trail system
attractions.hours: Parking Lot: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM daily; Historic Buildings: 9:00 AM - 4:00 PM daily; Visitor Center: 10:00 AM - 4:00 PM (Tuesday - Sunday); Park Trails: 6:00 AM - Sunset daily
attractions.admission: Free (Parking is $6 per vehicle, $5 for seniors)
highlights: The most fully restored mission in California with 37 furnished rooms
Over 25 miles of hiking and equestrian trails
Demonstration Days featuring mission-era crafts like candle making and weaving
Interactive Visitor Center and museum with Chumash and mission artifacts
Historic Mission Garden and restored livestock and poultry
Restored original aqueduct and washing pools
attractions.tips: Dogs are permitted on the multipurpose trails and roads but are not allowed in the historic buildings or the visitor center. Free guided tours are typically offered at 1:00 PM Wednesday through Sunday, with an additional 11:00 AM tour on Saturdays. Wear comfortable walking shoes as the main historic loop is a 1-mile walk on dirt pathways
attractions.nearby: Downtown Lompoc Murals, Lompoc Wine Ghetto, Jalama Beach County Park, La Purisima Golf Course, Vandenberg Space Force Base

attractions.name: Los Olivos, California
attractions.description: Los Olivos is a historic and charming wine village nestled in the heart of the Santa Ynez Valley, known for its Victorian architecture and vibrant arts scene. The town features nearly 30 wine tasting rooms, boutique shops, and chef-driven restaurants packed into a walkable few blocks. Originally a 19th-century stagecoach stop, it has evolved into a premier destination for wine enthusiasts and travelers seeking a relaxing California escape
attractions.address: Grand Avenue & Alamo Pintado Avenue, Los Olivos, CA 93441
attractions.hours: Town is accessible 24/7; most businesses (tasting rooms and shops) typically operate daily from 10:00 AM or 11:00 AM to 5:00 PM. Specific restaurant hours vary, such as Los Olivos Wine Merchant & Cafe: Sunday-Thursday 11:30 AM - 8:30 PM, Friday-Saturday 11:30 AM - 9:00 PM
highlights: Over 30 wine tasting rooms within a walkable downtown area
Historic 1918 flagpole located at the central intersection of Grand and Alamo Pintado Avenues
The historic Inn at Mattei's Tavern, a restored 1886 stagecoach stop
Boutique shopping at the Los Olivos General Store and unique art galleries
Access to the scenic 30-mile Foxen Canyon Wine Trail
Local artisan experiences including olive oil tastings and lavender farm visits
attractions.tips: Visit during the week for a more intimate wine tasting experience as weekends can draw large crowds. Wear comfortable walking shoes to explore the town's flat, walkable streets, and consider bringing a picnic to enjoy at one of the nearby winery grounds. Many tasting rooms close early (around 5:00 PM), so plan your evening dining in advance
attractions.nearby: Solvang (6 miles), Santa Ynez (5 miles), Ostrichland USA (10 miles), Quicksilver Ranch (3 miles), Cachuma Lake

attractions.name: Los Padres National Forest
attractions.description: Encompassing nearly two million acres, Los Padres National Forest spans the ruggedly beautiful landscapes of central California's Coast and Transverse Ranges. The Santa Barbara portion features the Santa Ynez Mountains, offering diverse ecosystems from chaparral to coniferous forests with breathtaking coastal vistas. It is a major destination for camping, hiking, and wildlife viewing, including habitat for the iconic California condor
attractions.hours: Office: Monday–Friday, 8:00 AM – 4:30 PM (Closed on federal holidays); Forest: Generally open 24 hours daily, subject to seasonal fire closures
attractions.admission: National Forest Adventure Pass: $5 per day or $30 annually. Lower Santa Ynez Recreation Area parking fee: $10 per day
highlights: Diverse ecosystems ranging from coastal chaparral to high-elevation conifer forests
Extensive trail network including popular routes like Tunnel Trail and Rattlesnake Canyon
Santa Ynez Recreation Area offering swimming, fishing, and picnicking along the river
Protected wilderness areas such as the Dick Smith Wilderness
Crucial habitat for endangered species including the California condor
Seasonal wildflower displays and natural hot springs
attractions.tips: Always check current fire restrictions and weather conditions before visiting. Carry adequate water, wear sturdy footwear for rocky terrain, and purchase a National Forest Adventure Pass in advance from local retailers. Cell reception is limited in many remote areas, so leave an itinerary with someone before heading out
attractions.nearby: Lake Cachuma, Nojoqui Falls Park, Solvang, Gaviota State Park, Santa Barbara Botanic Garden

attractions.name: Nojoqui Falls Park
attractions.description: Nojoqui Falls Park is a scenic 85-acre county park nestled in the rolling hills between Highway 101 and Solvang, best known for its seasonal 80-to-100-foot waterfall. Visitors can enjoy an easy 0.8-mile round-trip hike through a lush, shaded canyon filled with oak and sycamore trees. The park offers extensive amenities, including playgrounds, ball fields, and multiple picnic areas with BBQ grills, making it a popular destination for family outings and nature walks
attractions.hours: Monday: 8:00 AM - Sunset; Tuesday: 8:00 AM - Sunset; Wednesday: 8:00 AM - Sunset; Thursday: 8:00 AM - Sunset; Friday: 8:00 AM - Sunset; Saturday: 8:00 AM - Sunset; Sunday: 8:00 AM - Sunset
highlights: Seasonal 80-to-100-foot waterfall cascading over a mossy sandstone wall
Easy 0.8-mile round-trip shaded nature trail
Multiple reservable group picnic areas with BBQ grills
Two playgrounds for children and open ball fields
Rich history related to the Chumash people and early ranching
attractions.tips: The waterfall is seasonal and may dry up in late summer; the best time to visit is in winter or spring after rainfall. Wear sturdy shoes as the trail near the falls can be slippery, and be cautious of poison oak along the path. Note that there is very limited cell phone signal within the park canyon
attractions.nearby: Folded Hills Winery and Farmstead, Downtown Solvang, Old Mission Santa Inés, OstrichLand USA, Gaviota State Park

attractions.name: Old Mission Santa Inés
attractions.category: Landmark, Museum, and active Parish Church
attractions.description: Founded in 1804, Old Mission Santa Inés is the 19th of California's 21 missions and is celebrated as the "Hidden Gem of the Missions." The site features a beautifully restored 1817 adobe church, a museum housing a vast collection of 17th-century silk vestments and artifacts, and stunning gardens overlooking the Santa Ynez Valley
attractions.hours: Gift Shop: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM Daily; Museum/Self-Guided Tours: 9:00 AM – 4:30 PM Daily; Docent Tours (Scheduled): Wednesday – Monday, 10:00 AM – 2:00 PM; Daily Mass: 8:00 AM Monday – Saturday
attractions.admission: Self-Guided Tours: Adults (13+) $8.00, Seniors (64+) $7.00, Youth (6-12) $5.00, Children (5 and under) Free, Veterans and Active Military Free; Docent Led Tours (Advance booking required): Adults (18+) $12.00, Youth (6-17) $8.00
highlights: Beautifully restored 1817 Adobe Church with original wood-beam ceilings and colorful altarpiece
Extensive museum collection of 17th-century art, historical artifacts, and original mission bells
Madonna Chapel featuring period paintings and a dedicated wood-carved altar
Lush courtyard gardens with a central fountain and original seminary flooring
Historic cemetery serving as the final resting place for approximately 1,700 people
Breathtaking views of the Santa Ynez and San Rafael mountain ranges
attractions.tips: Plan for approximately 40-60 minutes to complete a self-guided tour. As the mission is an active parish, please remain respectful of worshippers in the chapel. Public restrooms are located at the back of the parking lot, as there are no public facilities inside the mission museum
attractions.nearby: Downtown Solvang (Danish Village), Hans Christian Andersen Museum and Park, Elverhoj Museum of History & Art, Solvang Festival Theater, and local Santa Ynez Valley wineries

attractions.name: Ostrichland USA
attractions.description: Ostrichland USA is a 33-acre farm and landmark that is home to over 150 giant birds, including more than 80 ostriches and 70 emus. Located between Solvang and Buellton, it offers a unique and fun experience where visitors can stand back and hand-feed these colossal birds. It is often described as a prehistoric park and a must-stop destination for families and photography enthusiasts ()
attractions.hours: Open Daily: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM (Gates close at 4:45 PM) ()
attractions.admission: General Admission: $7.00, Seniors (65+): $5.00, Military (with ID): $5.00, Children (12 & under): $3.00, Feed Bowl: $1.00 ()
highlights: Over 150 ostriches and emus to view and feed ()
Hand-feeding birds using specialized feeding bowls ()
Unique gift shop featuring ostrich-themed items and fresh ostrich eggs ()
Designated photo spots with murals and sculptures ()
Educational exhibits about bird behavior and history ()
Beautiful rustic landscape of the Santa Ynez Valley ()
attractions.tips: Visitors are advised to hold the feeding bowl close to the fence so the birds don't strain their necks, and to step away from the fence when not feeding to avoid being pecked. Bringing a couple of dollars extra for multiple bowls of feed is highly recommended, and the on-site gift shop sells ostrich and emu eggs when in season ()
attractions.nearby: Solvang Danish Village, Buellton, Sunny Fields Park, Hans Christian Andersen Park, Cachuma Lake Recreation Area ()

attractions.name: Rancho Sisquoc Winery
attractions.description: Rancho Sisquoc is a historic 37,000-acre working cattle ranch, grape grower, and winery located in the Santa Maria Valley. Established in 1972, the winery produces award-winning estate wines in small quantities and is known for its rustic charm and scenic vineyard views. Visitors can enjoy tastings in the original winery building or relax on the expansive picnic grounds. (,)

Tasting room housed in the original 1972 winery building ()

Pet-friendly environment ()

attractions.description: Founded in 1997, Rideau Vineyard is a boutique, family-owned winery known for its small-lot wines rooted in the traditions of the Rhône Valley. The estate is centered around the historic 1884 El Alamo Pintado Adobe and features fifteen acres of organically farmed vineyards. Visitors can enjoy tastings in a relaxed setting with lush gardens, century-old oak trees, and a seasonal food pairing menu

Established in 1995, it is dedicated to producing world-class, handcrafted wines that reflect the unique terroir of the region. The estate spans 48 acres and is known for its award-winning Syrah, Zinfandel, and Sauvignon Blanc, as well as Pinot Noir and Chardonnay sourced from top neighboring vineyards (,)

attractions.name: San Marcos Pass Road
attractions.description: San Marcos Pass Road, also known as State Route 154, is a breathtaking 32-mile scenic byway that traverses the rugged Santa Ynez Mountains. This historic route, once a stagecoach trail, offers panoramic views of the Pacific coastline, the Santa Barbara Channel Islands, and the sprawling vineyards of the Santa Ynez Valley. It provides a scenic alternative to Highway 101, winding through the chaparral-clad slopes of the Los Padres National Forest ()
attractions.address: State Route 154 (connecting Santa Barbara and Los Olivos), Santa Barbara, CA 93105
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours daily ()
highlights: Cold Spring Arch Bridge – A landmark engineering feat offering stunning canyon views ()

Chumash Painted Cave State Historic Park – Features 400-year-old Native American pictographs ()
Cold Spring Tavern – A historic 1860s stagecoach stop famous for its tri-tip sandwiches ()
Knapp's Castle – Accessible ruins of a historic sandstone mansion with sweeping mountain vistas ()
attractions.tips: This is a designated 'daylight-headlight' highway; ensure your headlights are on even during the day. The road is winding with significant elevation changes, so check brake conditions and watch for passing lanes to allow faster traffic to go by. Be sure to stop at the turnouts for the best photography spots overlooking Santa Barbara and the Pacific ()

attractions.name: Sanford Winery & Vineyards
attractions.description: Sanford Winery is a historic pioneer in the Sta. Rita Hills AVA, having planted the region's first Pinot Noir vines in 1971 at the iconic Sanford & Benedict Vineyard. The estate features a luxurious yet rustic mission-inspired tasting room and sustainably farmed vineyards that produce world-class Pinot Noir and Chardonnay. It gained widespread fame as a key filming location for the Academy Award-winning movie Sideways (,)

attractions.name: Santa Ynez Valley
attractions.description: Tucked between golden hills and purple horizons, the Santa Ynez Valley is the heart of Santa Barbara wine country. Across six distinct towns, visitors will find world-class wineries, Michelin-starred meals, small-town festivals, and secret trails. It offers a blend of rustic hospitality and sophisticated Central California living, just a short drive from the coast

highlights: Over 100 world-class wineries and tasting rooms
