Michelin-starred and farm-to-table dining experiences

Rich cultural heritage with historic landmarks and specialty museums

attractions.name: Solvang
attractions.description: Solvang is a historic Danish village in the Santa Ynez Valley that blends old-world Scandinavian charm with modern California wine country style. Visitors can stroll down cobblestone streets lined with windmills, European-style bakeries, and boutique shops while enjoying authentic Danish pastries and world-class wine tasting. The town is widely known as the "Danish Capital of America."

highlights: Authentic Danish architecture featuring four signature windmills
World-famous Danish bakeries serving Aebleskiver and traditional pastries

Founded in 1986 by Frank Ostini, it gained international fame as a primary filming location for the Academy Award-winning movie 'Sideways'. The restaurant specializes in meats, seafood, and local produce grilled over an open fire of red oak wood. (,)
attractions.hours: Wednesday - Monday: 4:30 PM - 9:00 PM (Last seating 8:30 PM); Bar opens at 4:00 PM; Tasting Room (Next Door): Wednesday - Sunday 11:30 AM - 5:00 PM; Lunch at Tasting Room: Wednesday - Sunday 11:30 AM - 3:00 PM; Closed Tuesdays ()
attractions.admission: Free entry; Dinner entrees generally range from ~$30 to $70+; Child's menu (under 12) is ~$15 (,)
highlights: Traditional Santa Maria-style BBQ grilled over an open red oak fire ()
Famous filming location for the 2004 movie 'Sideways' (,)
Award-winning Hartley Ostini Hitching Post Pinot Noir and other local wines ()
Open indoor barbecue kitchen where guests can watch the chefs at work ()
Signature appetizers like grilled artichokes with tomato ancho chile mayonnaise ()
Casual, rustic atmosphere with historic ranching photos and 'western charm' (,)
attractions.tips: Reservations are highly recommended, especially for dinner. Try the famous grilled artichoke and the signature Pinot Noir. If visiting for a more casual experience, consider 'Burger Nights' on Mondays and Wednesdays. For a more relaxed daytime experience, the tasting room next door offers lunch and wine flights with views of nearby ostriches. (,,)
attractions.nearby: OstrichLand USA, Hans Christian Andersen Park, Nojoqui Falls Park, Solvang Village, Santa Ynez Valley Botanic Garden (,,)

attractions.name: Zaca Mesa Winery & Vineyards
attractions.description: Founded in 1973, Zaca Mesa Winery & Vineyards is a family-owned estate and a pioneer of the Santa Barbara Rhône movement. As the first to plant Syrah in the county, the winery is dedicated to producing high-quality, terroir-driven Rhône-style wines. Visitors can enjoy tastings in a scenic courtyard overlooking the San Rafael Mountains or take private tours of the historic vineyard
attractions.address: 6905 Foxen Canyon Rd, Los Olivos, CA 93441
attractions.hours: Monday - Sunday: 11:00 AM - 4:00 PM
attractions.admission: Courtyard Tasting: $25; TREAD Flight: $30; Reserve Flight: $45; Private Winery Tour & Tasting: $95
highlights: First vineyard to plant Syrah in Santa Barbara County
Family and dog-friendly estate with a life-size outdoor chessboard
Home to the historic Black Bear Block, the oldest Syrah block on the Central Coast
Scenic courtyard with umbrellas for sunny days and heaters for breezy afternoons
Renowned "Zaca University" training ground for many famous winemakers
Diverse tasting flights including the TREAD label and estate reserves
attractions.tips: Reservations are highly recommended to guarantee seating, especially for Lounge and Terrace tastings. Outside food is welcome for picnics on the grounds when accompanied by a wine purchase. The morning can be cooler than the afternoon, so wearing layers is advised for outdoor tastings
attractions.nearby: Fess Parker Winery & Vineyard, Demetria Estate Winery, Firestone Vineyard, Foxen Vineyard & Winery, Los Olivos Downtown

attractions.name: Carpinteria Harbor Seal Rookery
attractions.description: The Carpinteria Harbor Seal Rookery is a protected coastal habitat where a colony of harbor seals returns annually to rest, mate, and give birth. Visitors can observe the seals from a dedicated overlook on the bluffs, providing a rare opportunity to see these marine mammals in their natural environment while maintaining a respectful distance. The site is one of the few remaining mainland rookeries in Southern California and is monitored by dedicated volunteers to ensure the animals remain undisturbed
attractions.hours: Sunrise to Sunset
highlights: Dedicated blufftop overlook for safe wildlife viewing
One of only four remaining harbor seal rookeries on the Southern California coast
Accessible via the scenic Carpinteria Coastal Vista Trail
Educational docents (Seal Watch) often present during pupping season
Adjacent to the Carpinteria Bluffs Nature Preserve and historic Casitas Pier
attractions.tips: The beach is strictly closed from December 1st to May 31st to protect pups; stay on the bluff trails during this time. Bring binoculars or a zoom lens for the best views without disturbing the seals. Keep noise to a minimum and keep dogs on a leash and away from the overlook area as seals are easily spooked. Early morning is often the best time to see the highest number of seals hauled out on the sand
attractions.nearby: Carpinteria State Beach, Tar Pits State Park, Carpinteria Bluffs Nature Preserve, Casitas Pier

attractions.name: Carpinteria State Beach
attractions.description: Carpinteria State Beach offers an iconic and diverse shoreline featuring white sand and rock outcroppings. It is a unique treasure with a rich biological and cultural history, where visitors can enjoy swimming, surf fishing, tidepool exploring, and camping in a small coastal community setting ()
attractions.hours: Monday: Sunrise - Sunset, Tuesday: Sunrise - Sunset, Wednesday: Sunrise - Sunset, Thursday: Sunrise - Sunset, Friday: Sunrise - Sunset, Saturday: Sunrise - Sunset, Sunday: Sunrise - Sunset
attractions.admission: Vehicle Day Use: $10.00,. Camping fees: Hike and Bike $10/night, No-hookup $45/night, Hookups $70/night
highlights: One mile of beach ideal for swimming and family recreation
Tidepool exploration featuring anemones, starfish, and sea urchins
Harbor Seal Rookery and sanctuary accessible via the Coastal Vista Trail
Tomol Interpretive Play Area and Visitor Center celebrating Chumash history
Beach-side camping with over 200 sites and modern facilities
attractions.tips: Dogs are permitted in the campground and day-use areas but are strictly prohibited on the beach (). Camping reservations are highly recommended and can be made up to six months in advance via Reserve California (). The beach's gentle slope makes it one of the safest for swimming, though visitors should always be mindful of ocean conditions ()
attractions.nearby: Carpinteria Tar Pits, Carpinteria Valley Museum of History, Lotusland, Santa Barbara Zoo

attractions.name: Bien Nacido Vineyards / The Gatehouse at Bien Nacido Estate
attractions.description: Bien Nacido Vineyards is an iconic, cool-climate vineyard in the Santa Maria Valley, renowned as one of California's most historic and respected wine estates. Established in 1973, it is a premier source of world-class Pinot Noir, Chardonnay, and Syrah for top winemakers across the state. Visitors can experience these exceptional wines at The Gatehouse, the estate's dedicated tasting room located directly on the vineyard property
attractions.address: 3503 Rancho Tepusquet Road, Santa Maria, CA 93454
attractions.hours: Monday: 11:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Tuesday: CLOSED; Wednesday: CLOSED; Thursday: 11:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Friday: 11:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Saturday: 11:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Sunday: 11:00 AM - 5:00 PM
attractions.admission: Estate Tasting Flight: Starting at $45; Burgundy Tasting Flight: Starting at $60; Rhone Tasting Flight: Starting at $60; Black Label Tasting Flight: Starting at $75; Adobe Experience: Starting at $100; Private Vineyard Tour and Tasting: Starting at $150
highlights: Historic vineyard founded in 1973 by the Miller family
World-renowned source for cool-climate Pinot Noir, Chardonnay, and Syrah
The Gatehouse estate tasting room featuring views of the rolling vineyards
Private electric all-terrain vehicle tours of the expansive property
Historic adobe on-site, built in 1857 by Don Juan Pacifico Ontiveros
Customizable sommelier-led tastings and diverse flight options
attractions.tips: Reservations are highly recommended for all tasting experiences and required for vineyard tours. The tasting room is closed on Tuesdays and Wednesdays, so plan your visit for the weekend or Monday/Thursday. For a more educational experience, consider booking a sommelier-led session or the Adobe Experience to learn about the site's 19th-century history
attractions.nearby: Cambria Estate Winery, Riverbench Vineyard and Winery, Presqu'ile Winery, Rancho Sisquoc Winery, Foxen Canyon Wine Trail, Los Flores Ranch Park

attractions.name: Buena Vista Park
attractions.description: Buena Vista Park is Santa Maria's oldest community park, originally established in 1906. It features a historic Camp Fire Cabin, basketball courts, and unique tile murals that depict the city's rich history. Following a major renovation in 2018, the park now offers modern amenities such as drought-tolerant gardens and a raised entertainment area for community events
attractions.address: 800 S Pine St, Santa Maria, CA 93454
attractions.hours: Monday: 6:00 AM - 8:00 PM, Tuesday: 6:00 AM - 8:00 PM, Wednesday: 6:00 AM - 8:00 PM, Thursday: 6:00 AM - 8:00 PM, Friday: 6:00 AM - 8:00 PM, Saturday: 6:00 AM - 8:00 PM, Sunday: 6:00 AM - 8:00 PM
highlights: Historic Camp Fire Cabin
Tile murals at the entrance depicting local history
Three basketball courts and tetherball areas
Expended multi-age playground and picnic area
Raised entertainment area for concerts and events
Drought-tolerant gardens and rolling landscapes
attractions.tips: Picnic tables and barbeque pits are available on a first-come, first-served basis. The park is dog-friendly, but pets must be kept on a leash and owners should clean up after them. Check the city calendar for special events and concerts held at the park's central bandstand
attractions.nearby: Santa Maria High School, Santa Maria Inn, Santa Maria Fairpark, Santa Maria Public Library, Santa Maria Valley Discovery Museum

attractions.name: Channel Islands Expeditions
attractions.description: Truth Aquatics, now operated by Channel Islands Expeditions, is a premier liveaboard and day-trip fleet providing access to the Channel Islands National Park. Utilizing the historic vessels Truth and Vision, they offer world-class kayaking through massive sea caves, kelp forest snorkeling, and wildlife discovery tours. The operation focuses on 'downwind' paddling expeditions, where the boat drops and picks up guests to ensure a smooth, one-way journey with the current
attractions.hours: Trips typically check in around 7:00 am - 8:00 am; office hours are generally 8:00 am - 5:00 pm daily
attractions.admission: $480 for Painted Cave Expedition (Paddlers aged 5+); Sea Cave Day Trips from $275 (Adults), $255 (Wiser Ones 65+ / Little Ones 5-17), and $225 for groups of 10-28
highlights: Expeditions to Painted Cave, one of the world's largest and deepest sea caves
Vessel-supported 'downwind' kayaking that eliminates the need to paddle against the wind
Kelp forest snorkeling with provided gear, including wetsuits, masks, and snorkels
Frequent sightings of whales and dolphins during the channel crossing
Full-day adventures including organic Mediterranean or BBQ lunches served on board
Historic liveaboard vessels (Truth and Vision) equipped for multi-day and single-day expeditions
attractions.tips: Book in advance as popular expeditions like the Painted Cave tour are seasonal and sell out quickly. Arrive early for check-in at the Santa Barbara Landing to ensure a smooth boarding process. Wetsuits and snorkeling gear are included in most day trips, but bring your own sunscreen and a camera for wildlife sightings
attractions.nearby: Santa Barbara Harbor, Stearns Wharf, Santa Barbara Maritime Museum, West Beach

attractions.name: Channel Islands Harbor
attractions.description: Channel Islands Harbor is a bustling recreational small-craft harbor and waterfront resort located in Oxnard, California. It serves as a major gateway to the Channel Islands National Park and features a diverse marketplace with dining, shopping, and maritime activities. The harbor includes over 2,000 boat slips, public parks, and several full-service marinas
attractions.hours: Public areas and parks: Open 24 hours daily. Robert J. Lagomarsino Visitor Center: 8:30 AM - 5:00 PM daily. Individual business and restaurant hours vary
highlights: Waterfront dining and shopping at Marine Emporium Landing
Whale watching and Channel Islands National Park excursions via Island Packers
Channel Islands Maritime Museum featuring maritime art and ship models
Weekly Sunday Farmers Market with fresh local goods
Extensive water sports including kayaking, jet skiing, and sportfishing
attractions.tips: Visit on Sundays from 10:00 AM to 2:00 PM to experience the popular Farmers Market. If you are planning to visit the National Park, it is recommended to book Island Packers ferry trips in advance. The harbor is also a great spot for sunset views and uncrowded beach strolls
attractions.nearby: Silver Strand Beach, Hollywood Beach, Kiddie Beach Park, Seabridge Marketplace, and Channel Islands National Park

attractions.name: Channel Islands National Park ()
attractions.description: Channel Islands National Park encompasses five remarkable islands and their surrounding ocean environment off the coast of Southern California (). Often called the "North American Galapagos," it preserves unique plants, animals, and archeological resources found nowhere else on Earth (). Visitors can experience the solitude of rugged sea cliffs, crystal-clear kelp forests, and ancient Chumash history ()
attractions.address: 113 Harbor Way 4th Floor, Santa Barbara, CA 93109 ()
attractions.hours: 10:30 am to 4:30 pm daily ()
attractions.days_open: Daily; closed on Thanksgiving Day, December 25, January 1, and the first Friday in August ()
attractions.admission: Free entry to the park (). Transportation fees apply: Island Packers ferry round-trip day tickets start at $63 for adults and $45 for children (). Camping fee is $15 per night ()
highlights: Dramatic ocean views from Inspiration Point on Anacapa Island ()
Sea cave kayaking to explore North America’s second-largest sea cave, Painted Cave ()
Wildlife viewing of endemic island foxes, massive seal and sea lion colonies, and migrating whales ()
Snorkeling and diving through pristine underwater kelp forests and volcanic reefs ()
Scenic hiking trails such as the Potato Harbor Overlook and Pelican Bay Trail ()
Preserved archeological sites and history of the Chumash people ()
attractions.tips: No services like food or water are available on the islands; visitors must pack in all supplies (). Transportation by ferry or plane should be booked months in advance through official concessionaires (). Island weather changes rapidly, so dressing in layers and checking current conditions is essential (). There are no motorized vehicles on the islands, so all exploration is on foot or by watercraft ()
attractions.nearby: Santa Barbara Maritime Museum, Stearns Wharf, Santa Barbara Waterfront, Santa Barbara Zoo, and the Santa Barbara County Courthouse ()

attractions.name: Cielito Lindo Mexican Grill
attractions.description: Cielito Lindo Mexican Grill is a beloved family-owned restaurant known for serving authentic Mexican cuisine with an emphasis on high-quality ingredients and fresh, bold flavors. The establishment offers a warm, family-friendly atmosphere where guests can enjoy traditional favorites like signature tacos and award-winning salsas. It has been a staple of the northern Santa Barbara County dining scene since opening its doors in 2001
attractions.address: 1130 E Clark Ave, Santa Maria, CA 93455
attractions.hours: Monday-Friday: 9:00 AM - 9:00 PM; Saturday: 8:00 AM - 9:00 PM; Sunday: 8:00 AM - 8:00 PM
highlights: Award-winning fresh salsa bar with multiple house-made varieties
Uses choice Angus meats in signature dishes like sizzling parrilladas
Voted "Best Authentic Mexican Food" by local readers
Famous for their Arroz con Pollo and Shrimp Cancun Enchiladas
Family-owned and operated for over two decades
Offers a casual, clean, and welcoming environment for families and travelers
attractions.tips: Don't miss the salsa bar with warm chips; the portions are known to be quite large, making many dishes suitable for sharing. The restaurant is a local favorite, especially for breakfast omelets and seafood enchiladas
attractions.nearby: Waller Park, St. Louis De Montfort Catholic Church, Old Orcutt Historic District

attractions.name: Gaviota State Park
attractions.description: Gaviota State Park is a sprawling 2,787-acre coastal park in southern Santa Barbara County featuring a mix of sandy beaches and rugged backcountry. It is famously marked by an iconic railroad trestle that spans Gaviota Creek and offers a diverse range of environments from oak forests to prairies. Visitors can enjoy activities such as swimming, surfing, and hiking to the 2,458-foot Gaviota Peak for panoramic views (,)
attractions.hours: 7:00 a.m. to Sunset, Daily ()
attractions.admission: $10.00 per vehicle for day use; $10.00 for Hike & Bike access; Camping fees are $45.00 per night ($35.00 in the off-season) (,)
highlights: Iconic Southern Pacific railroad trestle crossing Gaviota Creek
Gaviota Peak (2,458 ft) with expansive views of the coast and Channel Islands
Gaviota Hot Springs (sulfur springs) located a short hike from the trailhead
Gaviota Wind Caves featuring unique wind-carved sandstone formations
Secluded beach campground with 39 campsites near the ocean's edge
Gaviota Pier with a boat hoist for scuba divers and surfers
attractions.tips: Be prepared for extreme 'Sundowner' winds which can be hazardous for tent campers; check if the pier is open as it is frequently closed for storm damage; dogs are allowed in the campground and day-use areas but are strictly prohibited on the beach and trails; and mountain lion warnings are prominently posted in the backcountry (,)
attractions.nearby: El Capitán State Beach, Refugio State Beach, Gaviota Tunnel, Nojoqui Falls County Park, Solvang (,)

attractions.name: Hollister Ranch
attractions.description: Hollister Ranch is a 14,400-acre gated residential community and working cattle ranch located on the Gaviota Coast. It features nearly nine miles of pristine shoreline, secluded coves, and world-class surf breaks. The area is renowned for its environmental stewardship and remains one of the last undeveloped stretches of the Southern California coastline
attractions.hours: Access for residents is 24/7; Public groups by appointment or according to the community calendar
attractions.days_open: Daily for residents; Scheduled for approved groups
attractions.admission: Free for pre-approved educational/scientific groups; Private for owners/guests (State agencies are working on a public access program under AB 1680)
highlights: 8.5 miles of pristine Pacific Ocean shoreline
120+ miles of equestrian and hiking trails
World-class surf breaks including Cojo Point and Government Point
Diverse wildlife habitat with over 1,400 plant and animal species
Working cattle ranch with a 200+ year tradition
Historical Native American cultural sites
attractions.tips: Access is primarily restricted to property owners and their guests, but the 'wet beach' is state-owned and accessible by boat. Educational tours and the 'Tidepool School' are available for Santa Barbara-area schools and organizations. Hazards like collapsing cliffs and wild animals abound, so safety is paramount
attractions.nearby: Gaviota State Park, Point Conception, Jalama Beach County Park, Arroyo Hondo Preserve

attractions.name: Lompoc Flower Fields
attractions.description: The Lompoc Flower Fields offer a spectacular display of seasonal blooms in the "Valley of Arts and Flowers." From spring through fall, visitors can enjoy a patchwork of vibrant colors including Larkspur, Stock, and Delphinium grown by local commercial farmers. While the fields are privately owned, they can be beautifully viewed and photographed from the roadside
attractions.address: W Central Ave (between Floradale Ave and Union Sugar Ave), Lompoc, CA 93436
attractions.hours: Accessible 24/7; best viewed during daylight hours (no official operating hours as these are working farms)
highlights: Vibrant patches of seasonal blooms like Stock, Larkspur, and Delphinium
Located in the historic "Valley of Arts and Flowers"
Scenic DIY driving tours through rural agricultural fields
Heritage of the former "Flower Seed Capital of the World"
Annual Lompoc Flower Festival held every June
Nearby historic murals depicting the region's floral heritage
attractions.tips: Do not enter the fields as they are private property; photography must be from the roadside. Use the online 'Flower Tracker' on the Explore Lompoc website for the most current bloom information. Keep vehicles on paved roads and be mindful of heavy farm machinery and potential pesticide use
attractions.nearby: La Purisima Mission State Historic Park, Lompoc Murals, Sta. Rita Hills Wineries, Jalama Beach, Vandenberg Space Force Base

attractions.name: Oceano Dunes State Vehicular Recreation Area
attractions.description: Oceano Dunes State Vehicular Recreation Area is the only California State Park where vehicles may be driven and camped directly on the beach. This 3,500-acre park features expansive coastal dunes and offers a wide range of activities including off-roading, surfing, fishing, and horseback riding. It also serves as a critical habitat for protected shorebirds like the Western snowy plover and California least tern
attractions.hours: Vehicle Day Use: 7:00 AM – 10:00 PM; Camping: Check-in 2:00 PM, Check-out 12:00 PM
attractions.admission: Day Use: $5 per vehicle; Camping: $10 per night
highlights: Only state park in California allowing beach driving and camping
3,500 acres of expansive sand dunes for off-road exploration
Variety of recreational activities including surfing, fishing, and hiking
Home to the Oceano Dunes Visitor Center with hands-on nature exhibits
Critically important breeding site for the Western snowy plover and California least tern
attractions.tips: Four-wheel drive vehicles are highly recommended for driving on the sand. Drivers must observe a strict 15 mph speed limit and are required to have a whip and flag on their vehicles for visibility in the dunes. Always check tide charts before visiting, as the Arroyo Grande Creek crossing can become impassable during high tides or heavy rains
attractions.nearby: Pismo State Beach, Monarch Butterfly Grove, Oso Flaco Lake, Pismo Pier, Avila Beach

attractions.name: Pismo Beach
attractions.description: Pismo Beach is a classic California beach town located on the state's famous central coast, midway between San Francisco and Los Angeles. It is renowned for its long white sandy beaches, iconic 1,200-foot-long pier, and as the only state beach in California where vehicles can be driven on the sand. The town offers a mix of outdoor adventure, from surfing and kayaking to exploring the unique Oceano Dunes. (,)
attractions.hours: Beach and Pier: Open 24/7; Visitor Center: 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM Daily (,)
attractions.admission: Free; $5 for day-use parking at Pismo State Beach; vehicle access to sand is $5 per vehicle (,)
highlights: Historic 1,200-foot Pismo Beach Pier for sightseeing and fishing ()
Oceano Dunes State Vehicular Recreation Area for ATV riding and beach driving ()
Monarch Butterfly Grove attracting thousands of butterflies from October to February ()
Pismo Preserve with over 10 miles of hiking and biking trails ()
Award-winning clam chowder and local seafood scene ()
Coastal sea caves and cliffs accessible by kayak tours ()
attractions.tips: Check tide tables before exploring the sea caves or tide pools. Parking near the pier is paid and enforced from 10 AM to 6 PM. Dress in layers as coastal breezes can make it chilly, even in summer. (,)
attractions.nearby: Avila Beach, Oceano Dunes, San Luis Obispo, Shell Beach, Dinosaur Caves Park (,)

attractions.name: Pismo Beach Monarch Butterfly Grove
attractions.description: The Pismo Beach Monarch Butterfly Grove is one of the largest monarch colonies in the nation, hosting thousands of Western Monarchs annually. During the winter months, these vibrant orange and black butterflies cluster in the limbs of eucalyptus trees to seek shelter from northern winters. Visitors can witness this stunning natural display and learn about the monarch life cycle through docent talks and educational exhibits
attractions.hours: Open daily, dusk to dawn (weather permitting); Monarch Mercantile Trailer: 11:00 AM – 4:00 PM daily (extended to 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM during peak season, Oct-Feb)
highlights: One of the largest monarch colonies in the nation
Thousands of Western Monarchs clustering in eucalyptus trees
High-powered telescopes available for close-up viewing
Docent-led talks and educational videos via QR codes
Monarch Mercantile Trailer selling souvenirs to support conservation
Scenic trail connection leading to the beach and boardwalk
attractions.tips: The best time to see the butterflies is during the peak season from late October through February. For the best viewing, visit on sunny days when temperatures are above 55°F, as the butterflies will be in flight; on colder days, they remain in dense clusters that can look like dead leaves. Parking is very limited at the grove, so walking or biking from nearby areas is highly encouraged
attractions.nearby: Pismo State Beach, Oceano Dunes Natural Preserve, Pismo Pier and Boardwalk, Pismo Preserve, North Beach Campground

attractions.name: Santa Maria Valley
attractions.description: Santa Maria Valley is a premier Central Coast destination celebrated for its award-winning cool-climate wines and its 150-year-old Santa Maria Style barbecue tradition. The region offers a diverse array of outdoor activities, including exploring some of California's highest sand dunes and scenic cycling trails through rolling vineyards. It serves as an affordable home base for travelers looking to experience the authentic culture and natural beauty of northern Santa Barbara County
attractions.hours: Monday: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Tuesday: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Wednesday: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Thursday: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Friday: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Saturday: Closed, Sunday: Closed
highlights: Santa Maria Style Barbecue featuring tri-tip seasoned with a traditional dry rub and cooked over red oak
Foxen Canyon Wine Trail with 14+ world-class wineries and tasting rooms
Rancho Guadalupe Dunes Preserve featuring towering 550-foot-tall sand dunes
Santa Maria Valley Wine Trolley offering seasonal weekend transportation to local wineries
Los Flores Ranch Park with 1,800 acres of hiking, biking, and equestrian trails
Rich car culture and annual events like the West Coast Kustoms Cruisin’ Nationals
attractions.tips: Visit during the summer or fall to take advantage of the Santa Maria Valley Wine Trolley, which runs Friday through Sunday. For the most authentic dining experience, try the Santa Maria Style barbecue tri-tip paired with locally grown pinquito beans. March through November offers the most comfortable weather for exploring the dunes and hiking trails
attractions.nearby: Guadalupe-Nipomo Dunes Center, Oso Flaco Lake, Old Town Orcutt, La Purisima Mission State Historic Park, Pismo Beach

attractions.name: Vandenberg Space Force Base
attractions.description: Vandenberg Space Force Base is a premier United States Space Force installation and spaceport located on the Central Coast of California (). It serves as a critical site for satellite launches, missile testing, and space lift operations, encompassing nearly 100,000 acres and 42 miles of pristine coastline (). The base is the only U.S. facility capable of launching unmanned satellites into polar orbit and features the Space and Missile Heritage Center ()
attractions.address: 6 California Blvd, Building 17595, Vandenberg SFB, CA 93437 ()
attractions.hours: Visitor Center: Mon-Fri 6:00 AM - 6:00 PM, Sat-Sun Closed; SAMTEC Museum (2026 Spring): Thu-Fri 11:00 AM - 5:00 PM, 1st & 3rd Sat 11:00 AM - 5:00 PM; SLC-10 Landmark: Mon 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM ()
attractions.admission: Free (Museum and tours are generally free, though access is restricted and often requires prior appointment or military sponsorship) (,)
highlights: Active rocket launches by SpaceX, ULA, and NASA ()
Space and Missile Heritage Center (SAMTEC) museum ()
Space Launch Complex 10 (SLC-10) National Historic Landmark ()
42 miles of protected Pacific coastline ()
Strategic polar orbit satellite launch capability ()
Western Range mission control and tracking operations ()
attractions.tips: Access to the base is restricted to authorized personnel and sponsored guests; visitors must obtain a pass at the Visitor Center with a valid REAL ID and vehicle documentation (). It is highly recommended to check the launch schedule in advance, as launches are visible from various public vantage points in the surrounding Lompoc area (). Museum visits and historic site tours require prior appointment and security clearance ()
attractions.nearby: La Purisima Mission State Historic Park, Lompoc Murals, Solvang Danish Village, Ocean Beach Park ()
